programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Mickey Rooney guest stars as a comedian-turned-bookmaker who gets Kimble entangled in his troubles.

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble is mugged by three young toughs, but his passive reaction to the incident arouses the suspicion of a local reporter.

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble hitches a ride with a local clergyman, but finds himself in jeopardy when the cleric is killed by a sniper's bullet.

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team is asked to locate an endangered species of monkey living in the Amazon, to gain access to a unique antibody that could halt the spread of a dangerous virus

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Sloan's birthday turns out to be less than festive when he discovers the dead body of a prominent divorce lawyer in the hospital pharmacy. When Steve arrives, however, the evidence is nowhere to be found. Mark and Jack work feverishly to recover the missing body, find the culprit, and lead a tour of potential investors at the same time. Dick Van Dyke stars as the medical sleuth

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs's father is increasingly frustrated at being kept in his son's house under armed guard, and Franks joins his security detail after emerging from the convent where he had been hiding out. Meanwhile, Ducky finds evidence at a helicopter crash that gives the team an important lead in tracking down Paloma Reynosa, and the drug kingpin's brother Alejandro may have an ulterior motive when he tries to secure a deal involving her extradition. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team tries to locate a kidnapped girl but has to contend with her grandfather, a former member of a disbanded special forces unit. The agents discover he has been secretly negotiating with the kidnapper, who was once in his undercover team and has decided to kill off his old colleagues in a bid to atone for their actions. Meanwhile, Abby is forced to work with an intern, and makes him follow her idiosyncratic rules. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called to the home of a bomb disposal expert who shot and killed an intruder during a robbery. The case appears to be straightforward until Tony discovers she lied about being alone during the incident, and the evidence suggests a link to a case in which a man walked free after paralysing a member of the public in a shootout. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team becomes involved in an international incident when the body of an American petty officer is found by an estate agent in a bungalow that has been repossessed. An indentation on the victim's skull leads the agents to believe he was killed on a British ship, but collecting evidence from the vessel proves challenging. Guest starring Tracy Middendorf, Daniel Gillies and Kristen Ariza

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Happy's friend Ada, who lives in a sterile bubble that protects her compromised immune system, is left in danger when a severe storm hits

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team is asked to locate an endangered species of monkey living in the Amazon, to gain access to a unique antibody that could halt the spread of a dangerous virus

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Agents G Callen and Sam Hanna investigate after a naval officer is killed during an LAPD shootout, with two other men who died in the gun battle being identified as members of a Mexican drug cartel. Spin-off drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects, an elite NCIS division specialising in undercover assignments. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Three men stage a raid on a drug deal, taking a suitcase full of cash and killing the dealer and an undercover LAPD officer. While studying footage taken from CCTV equipment in the house, Sam recognises the distinctive hand signals of one of the suspects as being those of Navy Seal Kurt Holgate, who he served with in Bosnia. Eric studies the details of his unit and finds that a man called Jackson has been unaccounted for since the day of the attack. Drama, starring LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: On a live-fire air force training exercise, someone hacks into the control systems of a drone and fires one of its two live missiles, striking and killing a serviceman, before piloting it away. Sam and Callen meet with a member of the National Security Agency, while Eric's inquiries lead him to a group of students on an advanced computer science course and Kensi is sent under cover to pose as its guest speaker

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A man who stole a car and ran down and killed one of his pursuers before escaping is identified as Walton Flynn, an ex-marine now working for a private security firm in Iraq. Sam and Callen learn that the businessman he had been hired to protect had been murdered and that the people chasing him were hired by his employers to dish out their own form of justice. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A jet-skier crashes on to the shore and is discovered to be dead, having been shot through the chest. At HQ, Hetty shows the team surveillance footage of the shooting and identifies the victim as Daniel Su, an engineer who defected from North Korea and was working for the US government on a highly classified research project. Sam and Callen visit the victim's apartment, where they find the body of a woman on the floor

programme.title: Matlock

programme.title: Mission: Impossible
programme.desc: The team tries to discover the number of a Swiss bank account by convincing the holder that the Third World War has started

programme.title: Bondi Rescue
programme.desc: The start of school holidays at Bondi sees lifeguards inundated with over-excited children throwing caution to the wind in the surf. A few new youngsters have joined the Bondi's lifeguard team, including 16-year-old Trent Maxwell. Christmas Day brings the madness and mayhem of surf and alcohol, and lifeguards are called on to perform life and death rescues in big seas

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the deaths of a radio DJ and a naval officer who were both killed during a live broadcast, only to discover there are a number of potential suspects. Evidence leads the agents to suspect the involvement of a radical group called Military at Home, prompting Tony to investigate an upmarket gated community - where his door-to-door inquiries yield an enticing proposition or two from the female residents. American drama, starring Michael Weatherly, with Nadia Bjorlin

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Logan and Briscoe investigate the murder of a young, independent deaf woman, and start by questioning her two most recent boyfriends. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Lt Col Mann arrives to assist with inquiries into the murder of a marine - a case that becomes personal for Gibbs when he learns one of the witnesses is his ex-wife Stephanie. The team discovers the prime suspect was having an affair with the dead man's spouse, but the case is complicated by the revelation that the victim may have been selling top-secret military information. Starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The Rangers investigate a series of accidents involving a Cherokee Indian racing team. Trent and Carlos save a young girl from a paedophile

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team tries to track down wanted terrorist Harper Dearing before another US Navy ship is destroyed, and a desperate Gibbs enlists the aid of a prisoner to draw his target out of hiding. Meanwhile, Palmer is forced to consider putting his nuptials on hold as the stand-off with Dearing becomes heated. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine Brandon Valdivia falls to his death from a rooftop bar and is initially thought to have committed suicide, but his family refuse to accept that possibility and persuade a congressman to put some pressure on the admiral. Sam and Callen are then brought in to re-evaluate the case and discover that Valdivia may have been involved in money laundering with a fellow marine.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While performing an autopsy for a class of military students, Ducky discovers the cadaver he is using is the body of a murder victim. In trying to identify the corpse, Gibbs and his team are set on the trail of a career criminal specialising in identity fraud. Meanwhile, Ziva has difficulty separating her personal feelings and professional judgment. Starring David McCallum and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The aftermath of Harper Dearing's bomb attack on the NCIS headquarters dominates proceedings. As rescue crews sift through the debris searching for survivors, Abby, Gibbs, McGee and Vance are accounted for, but Ziva and Tony are trapped in a lift. Dearing makes contact after his ex-wife is taken into custody, enabling his car to be traced to an abandoned lot - and the agents arrive just in time to see the vehicle blow up. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A woman is shot dead in an apartment while keeping watch on a man on a yacht in a marina, and the killer takes her camera and computer. A flash drive is recovered from the victim's baseball cap and surveillance footage on it includes images of Callen, who deduces that the pictures were taken in the days leading up to the attempt on his life and that the dead woman was the last person to see him before he was shot

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.desc: Examining evidence that Russian princess Anastasia may have survived the execution of her family during the revolution. Plus, the case of a seductive confidence trickster

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Will gets involved in an affray outside a nightclub and finds his career is on the line - even though the incident gives the team enough evidence to foil a raid on a jeweller's shop. Zain's father is arrested on suspicion of burglary, but it turns out he is on a more high-minded mission on behalf of a friend. After a night of passion, Gina discovers her new conquest is a married man

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Jack becomes impatient for a result in the undercover operation to trap drug dealer Jose Alvarez, and Zain panics when he's pressed to set up a sting which would bring Kristen Shaw down as well. Roger and Dan deal with a troublesome family who intend to spend their lottery win on one big party and Emma is left holding the baby, while James Tennant prepares for his court appearance by getting blind drunk

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: With the Serious Organised Crime Agency muscling in on his case, Jack orders Zain to take a break and brings Kristen Shaw in for questioning, but he's forced to let her go when she refuses to name the big boys in the Colombian drugs operation. Dan is sent home when Gina learns his steroid use caused him to lose control in the boxing match, leaving his opponent struggling for life. Lottery winners the Hendersons provide further proof money can't buy happiness

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Stuart and Terry are surprised to discover ex-colleague DC Jo Masters working undercover when they investigate a counterfeit satellite navigation systems operation, and learn that they have stumbled upon a much bigger crime involving ruthless gun-runners. Honey and Leela sort out a scuffle at a funeral involving feuding brothers and end up falling for the same smooth-talking Irishman

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: Four-part adaptation of Charlotte Bronte's novel about an orphan girl who struggles to survive the harsh treatment of a boarding school to embark on a career as a governess. Jane takes up her first position at the home of the alluring and unpredictable Edward Rochester, under the watchful eye of laundrywoman Grace Poole. Ruth Wilson, Toby Stephens and Francesca Annis star

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: Jane begins to wonder about the chemistry between herself and Rochester, who disappoints her when he leaves without warning. However, he returns with a houseful of distinguished guests, including the beautiful Blanche Ingram and her opinionated mother. Things turn sour when a mysterious visitor arrives. Ruth Wilson, Toby Stephens and Francesca Annis star

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: After fulfilling her dying aunt's last wishes, Jane returns to Thornfield Hall - only to find Mr Rochester is set to wed Blanche. However, after she confesses her love, he shares his true feelings and asks for her hand in marriage instead. While all seems well at first, a ghostly figure begins to torment Jane as the wedding day draws near. Drama, with Ruth Wilson and Toby Stephens

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: A clergyman and his two sisters take a heartbroken Jane under their wing after finding her alone and starving on the moors. The governess embarks on a new life as a parish teacher, but thinks she hears Rochester call out her name and feels compelled to return to Thornfield - where the secrets of the North Tower are finally revealed. Starring Ruth Wilson and Toby Stephens

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Tide of Life
programme.desc: Gillian Kearney stars in the feature-length period drama following the fortunes of housekeeper Emily Kennedy and her relationships with three very different men. At first she is content working for the McGilby family while her father is away at sea, but finds her life thrown into turmoil by the death of one of her employers. With John Bowler and Ray Stevenson

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Barbara faces her most challenging case so far when she treats a first-time mother, only for the birth not to go according to plan. A new nurse arrives and soon makes an impression on Sister Evangelina, while Sister Julienne is worried about the increasing financial pressure on Nonnatus House, but doesn't bank on a mystery benefactor coming to the rescue. Jenny Agutter and Pam Ferris star, with Linda Bassett (East Is East) joining the cast

programme.title: Les Misérables
programme.desc: A desperately poor Fantine is forced to flee Paris in search of work, and is left contemplating a terrible decision that could ensure her daughter enjoys a brighter future. Her fate takes an even darker turn as she struggles to meet the increasingly outrageous demands of the Thenardiers. Valjean settles into his new life as a prosperous factory owner, but his newfound happiness is threatened when Javert is appointed as the town's new police inspector, and remains as determined as ever to expose him

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Anne Boleyn summons Cromwell, anxious to know whether she has an ally in him, and he witnesses her venting her frustrations on her ladies-in-waiting. The king starts to take notice of Cromwell and comes to rely on the lawyer's advice, but then news arrives from York that Cardinal Wolsey has been arrested by Harry Percy, a vengeful act for denying his betrothal to Anne eight years earlier. Historical drama adapted from Hilary Mantel's Booker prize-winning novels Wolf Hall and Bring Up the Bodies, starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis, Claire Foy and Jonathan Pryce

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: DI Pat North is assigned to look into a case that may clear a man convicted of murder eight years ago, and finds that DS Walker was in charge of the original inquiry. Drama, starring Kate Buffery, David Hayman and James Wilby

programme.title: Wuthering Heights
programme.desc: First in a two-part adaptation of Emily Bronte's classic tale. In 1815, Mr Earnshaw returns home from Liverpool with orphan Heathcliff, who befriends his daughter Cathy but is resented by his son Hindley. Upon his father's death 10 years later, Hindley inherits Wuthering Heights and forces Heathcliff to live in the stables, while Cathy must choose between a life of comfort and her love for Heathcliff. Tom Hardy, Charlotte Riley, Andrew Lincoln and Sarah Lancashire star

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Hugh becomes the town hero after he saves a footballer from a booby-trapped paddock, but is soon brought back down to reality

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sisters Sharon and Tracey's lives are changed for ever when their husbands leave for work and never return. First episode of the popular comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: The sisters face up to the realities of life with their husbands in prison, and try to explain things to Tracey's son, Garth. Classic comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Reg and Tony are called to assist a council bailiff trying to evict a pregnant woman from her flat, but end up arresting the official for violent conduct. Jo's undercover operation to snare the gun-runners takes a sinister turn when supplier Philip Hanson is found dead in the boot of his car. Leela admits to Honey she took Irish charmer Brad Durkin home the previous night

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Maria is concerned that Sam's cancer has returned when he starts acting strangely. Joseph tries to convince himself that nothing happened between himself and Jac, and Kyla hands in her notice so she can join Max in Holland. Ric is under pressure to find a new anaesthetist, and Linden is once again too heavily involved with his patients' lives as he deals with an apparent love triangle

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: While rushing to work, Harry accidentally knocks down a cyclist who is left seriously injured _ and the tragic incident finally forces him to confront his escalating problems. Tess overhears Roxy discussing her pregnancy, causing all manner of strife, and Mike is given the hospital cleaner's job after complaints about the department's lack of hygiene

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Diane encounters an old army colleague and the incident stirs painful memories of her service days when a young soldier committed suicide. Smithy lends a sympathetic ear when she confides in him about her guilty feelings at having failed to halt the bullying which caused the tragedy. Mickey pushes the boat out to capture Mia's heart and Honey invites Will back to her place for the night after an evening in the pub

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ricky and Bianca take the plunge, Steve finally meets his match, and Grant has to face some home truths

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant and Phil spring a welcome surprise on Peggy, Bianca hides the truth from Carol, Pat's suspicious of David's behaviour and Mark has a secret of his own

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah and George begin a secret affair and meet at the cottage. However, Anna hopes that if she can push her father towards Sarah that he will be more open to learning about her relationship with Gino, but she is frustrated over the arrival of her aunt Regina. Meanwhile, James struggles with his emotions. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Tom Deneley, still grieving over Joanna's death, agrees to help out at the surgery until baby Joe is well enough to leave hospital, while newcomer Alex Redman also joins the team. A patient suspected of being a paedophile causes a fierce dispute among the medics, while elsewhere in Cardale, Sam's discovery of a dark family secret presents him with a dilemma. Gary Mavers, Gray O'Brien, Joseph Millson and Maggie O'Neill star

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Christmas in Skeldale brings no respite from animal emergencies, as pampered pooch Tricky Woo falls ill and Mrs Pumphrey entrusts him to Helen's care. Christopher Timothy and Carol Drinkwater star

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: William wins the respect of the locals when he proves himself adept at the French game of boules. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: A marriage proposal leads to Diana being loaded into an ambulance, and Harvey becomes set on the idea of becoming a priest. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth visits a stately home, where she naturally tries her hardest to meet the lord of the manor - only to be thwarted by the untimely arrival of Onslow and Daisy. Suburban comedy, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Truly and Clegg are enlightened in the wisdom of the East by an electrical handyman from Hull, and Howard struggles to keep his self-styled bandit persona alive. Meanwhile, a debt-collector is an unwelcome guest for Tom

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Rosemary's old school friend Dorothy invites the duo to restore the gardens of her beautiful villa on the French Riviera so the property can be opened to the public. The plan arouses bitter hostility among the neighbours and long-held family secrets start to surface, leading to murder. Drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A popular reggae band reunite after 30 years to perform on Saint Marie, but the joyous occasion is blighted when guitarist Billy Springer is found dead in his dressing room afterwards. With a gun in his hand and the door locked from the inside, all evidence points toward suicide - but when the Commissioner reveals he received a message from the victim earlier in the day, the case becomes rather more complicated. Meanwhile, Dwayne struggles to come to terms with his dad's surprise visit. Is it too late for them to make amends? With guest appearances by Ram John Holder, Suzanne Packer, Delroy Brown and Levi Roots

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The discovery of a Roman sword with traces of blood on it is linked to an unidentified, headless corpse found near Heathrow in March 2008. It leads the team members to a lock-up belonging to a bodybuilder called Mark Rix, who died of a suspected heart attack the same year, so they start looking for connections between the original murder and Rix's death. They are helped in their investigation by anthropologist Fiona Kennedy, for whom Dan begins to develop feelings - just as he is told his wife might be discharged from the secure hospital where she has spent the past several years. Tracy-Ann Obermann and Liz White guest star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Harry suspects a cover-up when the government releases its report on the deaths of seven terror suspects killed in an alleged accidental prison fire. However, his failure to act in time leads to one of his team being framed for their murder. Drama, guest starring Tim McInnerny, with Peter Firth and Rupert Penry-Jones

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Diane encounters an old army colleague and the incident stirs painful memories of her service days when a young soldier committed suicide. Smithy lends a sympathetic ear when she confides in him about her guilty feelings at having failed to halt the bullying which caused the tragedy. Mickey pushes the boat out to capture Mia's heart and Honey invites Will back to her place for the night after an evening in the pub

programme.title: Wuthering Heights
programme.desc: Conclusion. Tormented by his love for Cathy, Heathcliff arrives at Thrushcross Grange the day after her wedding to Edgar. Tension mounts as the pair acknowledge their passion for one another, but when she falls pregnant with Edgar's child, Heathcliff leaves Gimmerton to marry Isabella. Tom Hardy, Charlotte Riley, Andrew Lincoln, Rosalind Halstead and Burn Gorman star in Emily Bronte's classic tale

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: A marriage proposal leads to Diana being loaded into an ambulance, and Harvey becomes set on the idea of becoming a priest. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole

programme.desc: Sharon spreads her wings and takes full advantage of her sister's hospitality, but Tracey is having money worries. Ross Kemp guest stars, with Linda Robson and Pauline Quirke

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey makes an appointment with a Harley Street specialist, hoping to allay her suspicions that she may be pregnant. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Diane encounters an old army colleague and the incident stirs painful memories of her service days when a young soldier committed suicide. programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric learns a drug-addicted patient was his late son Leo's partner. As Sam gets to know Kieron, he comes to realise what sacrifices his son is prepared to make for their relationship. It is Kyla's last day and Max has a surprise in store for her, while Linden begins to lose faith after spending the night at Callie's bedside. Daisha makes a big decision about baby Joe

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Harry misreads Lara's signals at the calendar launch, leading to an ill-advised attempt to kiss her. Roxy confesses to being a surrogate mother and Comfort is led astray by a friend out for a good time, but a prank involving a stolen car has fatal consequences. Jim gets down on bended knee to propose to Nikki and a paranoid Mike decides to quit when embarrassingly faced with an old colleague

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A Turkish man reports his 16-year-old daughter missing, but further inquiries reveal she is happily living with her boyfriend and has no intention of returning to a life of drudgery and exploitation. Stuart takes over the case when the worried father denounces the boy's family for setting up illegal marriages for immigrants in return for cash. Mickey's old flame Carrie Morgan turns up at Sun Hill accused of theft by her employer, and the harassed detective realises he must distance himself from the ex-prostitute if he wants to make his relationship with Mia work

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Sam tries her hand at pulling pints and receives unexpected attention from Ricky. David attempts to turn the tables on Grant and Phil by talking of blackmail

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Peggy lays down the law with her family, while Pat's warnings to David go unheeded. Sam's appearance at the Quiz Night causes tensions to explode, and Carol gets a nasty shock

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: George is asked to host a Japanese trade delegation on the eve of Remembrance Day. Knowing how the community will react, he asks Jack to come to the reception in the hope that the approval of a former Japanese prisoner of war might help deal with any ill feeling. At the event Regina is openly hostile toward Sarah, which she attributes to anti-Semitism. Anna's prolonged absence from Ash Park has Gino fearing that their relationship is over, but he is unaware that she is with Caroline awaiting the results of a pregnancy test. Meanwhile, James and Olivia continue to grow closer. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: New recruit Alex tries to get to the bottom of a local policeman's aggressive behaviour and is disturbed to discover he has a drug habit, while Richard edges ever closer to finding out the truth about Kate's affair with Andrew. Elsewhere, Tom sets the cat among the pigeons when he is forced to break some bad news. Medical drama, starring Gary Mavers and Maggie O'Neill

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: The vets have to contend with an outbreak of illness on the grouse moors, and James tries to save a cow from slaughter. Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison star

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: Hester and William rely on their son-in-law's knowledge of French law when they both find themselves in trouble with the authorities. Julia McKenzie and Anton Rodgers star

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom sets out to prove himself by learning to drive and Harvey again applies for membership of the golf club, masquerading as a clergyman. Sitcom set in the Bayview Retirement Village, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth feels harassed by a combination of working at a charity shop, trying to impress Councillor Nugent and worrying about Rose's love life. To cap it all, Daisy informs her that a strange woman in Daddy's bedroom claims to be his fiance. Starring Patricia Routledge

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: After helping Entwistle repair his truck, Truly, Clegg and Billy find their mechanical skills are put to the test once more when they help Tom and Smiler locate the source of an irritating squeak on their handcart. However, when Barry's longed-for game of golf evaporates thanks to his borrowed car breaking down, all the trio can do is help him drown his sorrows

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates a friend's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train, but everyone is initially stumped by the lack of a body. Feature-length drama, starring Joan Hickson, David Horovitch, Joanna David and Jill Meager

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The arrest of a Turkish girl who has been working illegally leads Ucos to reopen the case of Richard Gibson, a pub landlord who died in a fire in 2009. The original investigation concluded Gibson's death was probably suicide due to his dire financial situation, but the detectives have some questions about the pub's live-in barmaid, who disappeared on the night of the blaze. Could widowed landlady Joanne, pub regular Jason, or the dead man's best friend David, who happens to have a sideline in passing off his British-produced sparkling wine as French champagne, supply them with any answers? While the case tests the team's brain power, Sasha is also keen to raise their fitness levels as she signs them up for a five-a-side football competition. Phil Davis, Niamh Cusack and Tom Georgeson guest star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Part one of two. While Adam and Zaf hunt down an elite team of terrorists who have entered Britain, Ros is assigned to safeguard a delegation of Middle Eastern princes taking part in a secret government deal. However, things take a turn for the worse when the Saudi Trade Centre is stormed after news of the transaction is leaked. Rupert Penry-Jones and Raza Jaffrey star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A Turkish man reports his 16-year-old daughter missing, but further inquiries reveal she is happily living with her boyfriend and has no intention of returning to a life of drudgery and exploitation. Stuart takes over the case when the worried father denounces the boy's family for setting up illegal marriages for immigrants in return for cash. Mickey's old flame Carrie Morgan turns up at Sun Hill accused of theft by her employer, and the harassed detective realises he must distance himself from the ex-prostitute if he wants to make his relationship with Mia work

programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Live Women's FA Cup Football
programme.desc: Everton v Manchester City (Kick-off 2.30pm). Gabby Logan presents all the action from the final at Wembley Stadium. Everton are looking to lift the trophy for the first time since the 2009/10 season and have impressed since the competition resumed with victories over Chelsea and Everton. The Citizens have overcome Leicester City and Arsenal since the restart and will now have high hopes of winning the trophy for the third successive season

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers compete to design a garden for a family of seven in Stamford, Lincolnshire, who have just moved from rural Rutland and miss the countryside. With five noisy children, the parents want a peaceful garden that they can call their own, but the awkward plot gives the presenters a huge challenge, despite a budget of £4,500

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In the sixth annual event, the team heads out across the UK to lead rambles and meet inspirational youngsters who have been helped by the charity. Matt Baker is in the Peak District to take on challenge with 16-year-old Harvey, who was supported by bereavement counselling after his brother was killed, while Steve Brown is inspired to attempt scaling Wales' Pen y Fan mountain in his wheelchair after meeting 5-year-old Emerson, a sport-lover with a spinal-cord injury

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from the first two live weekends. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off before Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel-Horwood and Motsi Mabuse decide who will be the first pair to leave the contest. With music by Sam Smith

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness introduces Chris Harris and Freddie Flintoff to his latest creation - an all-electric, all-terrain ice-cream van for the 21st century. Elsewhere, the team tests the battery-powered Honda E, Vauxhall Corsa-e and Mini Electric with a race around a deserted Alton Towers

programme.title: Roadkill
programme.desc: Confronted about his affair, pressure mounts on the home front for Peter, while the party chairman asks him about his ambitions. Is he after the top job? Rochelle decides to pick up where Charmian left off

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, introducing highlights of Manchester United v Arsenal at Old Trafford and Tottenham Hotspur v Brighton & Hove Albion at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. Plus, action from Aston Villa v Southampton at Villa Park and Newcastle United v Everton at St James' Park

programme.title: Women's Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the final round of fixtures in the annual international rugby union tournament, featuring France v Ireland, Wales v Scotland and Italy v England

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Crewmates Adam and Dan treat a patient who has fallen down a flight of stairs in a casino, while Rachel and Stuart respond to a call when a child falls from a bunk bed. As the shift continues, a distressing call from a patient threatening to jump into the river takes priority, and the call handler must try to keep the patient talking until help can arrive

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the progress of a mid-terrace house in Chatham, Kent, a detached house in Bilston, West Midlands and a piece of land in Chesterton, Newcastle-under-Lyme

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers inspect takeaways in Oldham to ensure the food is safe to eat, make sure everyone gets home safely in Newcastle and crack a gold bangle scam in Birmingham

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A young man lies unconscious in his bedroom and it is up to an on-line friend in Texas to raise the alarm. Plus, a nine-year-old boy on holiday is stung by a giant jellyfish

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roo Irvine and Danny Sebastian help two teams hunt for antiques at the West Midlands Showground in Shrewsbury, while Natasha Raskin Sharp tests the design knowledge of fairgoers

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: The medical drama returns after a break due to the pandemic. The gang celebrates Jimmi's birthday, while Rob drives Karen and Jay up the wall, and Deborah encounters a stubborn mother-to-be

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin puts Tunbridge Wells and its property market to the test with his first- time buyers, who have £425,000 to invest in a starter home after saving hard for the deposit

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades transforms a stained-glass door and window, Rachel Henderson takes an old fireplace, and Mark Haig attempts to inject new life into a collection of brass lighting

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents a programme in which five dealers bid against one another to buy intriguing items brought in by members of the public. The opening edition features an unusual item of hairdressing memorabilia, something salvaged from a tip, a classic piece of industrial furniture and a beloved toy

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The Chinese video-sharing service was the social media sensation of lockdown, an app that provided a bit of fun for people stuck indoors and now has hundreds of millions of users. In this report, Tina Daheley investigates whether it is safe for the millions of young people who have signed up to use it

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jack tells Denise that four of Ellie's properties have been raided by the police - but he is convinced Phil had something to do with it. Jay goes against Honey's wishes and tells Billy what happened, and when they discover that her attacker has been released on bail, Billy's emotions get the better of him. Rainie and Stuart are in dire need of a new member of staff. Luckily for them, Tiffany is in need of part-time work

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Porridge star Mark Bonnar, actress Sheila Hancock, actor Stephen Mangan and radio and TV presenter Anita Rani join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show in which participants attempt to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Silent Witness star Liz Carr traces her family tree, learning about the life of her maternal grandfather, who died when her mother was just two. She also discovers that her paternal great-great-great-grandfather was involved in the attempted murder of a prominent landlord in Armagh in the 1850s

programme.title: Stand Up for Live Comedy
programme.desc: Jamali Maddix hosts the showcase for emerging comic talent from London, kicking things off with his experiences of 'original' Covid. He then introduces three guests, with Michael Odewale enlightening the audience about reverse revenge and black-on-black crime, Eleanor Tiernan admitting to overthinking everything and having a weird personality, and Helen Bauer rounds off the evening with tales of eating her own body weight in cheese

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: Peter Kay and Sian Gibson return for a one-off special episode of the comedy about the daily commute of supermarket employees John and Kayleigh, with the two stars taking the plunge and improvising the dialogue for the whole show

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Steph McGovern hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Miles Jupp and Sayeeda Warsi joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham is joined by Irish actor and singer Jessie Buckley, starring in Charlie Kaufman's Netflix drama I'm Thinking of Ending Things. Also appearing are comedian Bill Bailey - one of this year's Strictly contestants - plus fellow comic and children's author David Walliams. Singer Dermot Kennedy performs his single Giants, and the host offers a wry look at some trending news stories

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell explore a quirky cottage in Paddock Wood, Kent, a new build in Gnosall, Staffordshire, and a one-bedroom flat in Northampton

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: A fly-tipper who illegally dumped tonnes of waste in the grounds of an Oldham primary school for children with learning difficulties, leaving the school with a £22,000 bill

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A teenager fights to save her father's life when he has a heart attack, and a driver loses control of a car, scooping two men up on to the bonnet and crashing through a shop window

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Gary Pe and Nick Hall help two teams search for items in the West Sussex town of Ardingly. Presenter Anita Manning also delves into restoration by learning how to gild a bauble

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Deborah is left out in the cold, and Sid may be the only one who can help her. Daniel, Zara and Harper make an uneasy threesome, and Bear tries to help a friend in need

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Presenter Steve Brown helps a bachelor find an ideal property in Guildford, Surrey, on a budget of £420,000

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and the team are at Wallyford Recycling Centre in East Lothian, where they transform a nest of tables, a tired trolley, and a telephone table

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against one another to buy intriguing items brought in by members of the public, including a selection of Hornby model railway equipment. Nigel Havers presents

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jay is amazed with Honey's strength after she talks to the police again, while Mick is unimpressed when Max offers Linda another shift at the restaurant. Kat comes up with a brainwave to sort out the Slaters' money woes - but Stacey and Kush are not best pleased with the idea. Tiffany is overwhelmed with the more morbid aspects of her new job at the funeral parlour but Rainie and Stuart do their best to make her feel welcome as only they can

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: Ten well-known actors come together to record an album for Children in Need. Cameras follow Helena Bonham Carter, Jim Broadbent, Olivia Colman, Shaun Dooley, Luke Evans, Suranne Jones, Adrian Lester, Himesh Patel, David Tennant and Jodie Whittaker over 12 weeks in the summer of 2019 as they rehearse songs for their album, Got It Covered. Each actor must choose a song that has special meaning for them and must make their cover version their own. The participants also visit some charity projects that Children in Need supports in order to help raise funds and awareness

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: Hannah's wedding day arrives, bringing everyone's stories together. David explores memories of his own marriage, Belle makes an unlikely alliance, Hannah confronts the question that has been plaguing her ever since Andy came back into her life, and Gail realises she may be the only one who can help

programme.title: Catch Her If You Can
programme.desc: Documentary following an attempt to expose a successful London businesswoman's double life as a con artist. A suspicious invoice tipped off one of her potential victims, and inspired her to track down other parties who had been fleeced by her in the past. Together, they came up with a plan to expose her misdeeds

programme.title: Rent Like a Boss
programme.desc: Big V and Tobias give their advice on outrageous rental issues, from dealing with nosy neighbours to landlords who do not respect their tenants' privacy. They also help a Manchester resident looking for a flatshare, who insists on bringing a caravan full of theatrical costumes with him to his new home

programme.title: US Election 2020
programme.desc: Katty Kay and Andrew Neil present rolling coverage of the US election result, as either Donald Trump or Joe Biden will be confirmed as president. Trump and vice president Mike Pence are hoping to hold onto the positions they have held for the Republican Party since the last election in 2016, while Barack Obama's former VP Biden and running mate Kamala Harris are looking to regain Democratic control of the White House

programme.title: US Election 2020
programme.desc: Katty Kay and Andrew Neil present rolling coverage of the US election result

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood's alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture continues with the letter M. Various BBC gardening presenters are mesmerised by magnolias, marvel at the monkey puzzle tree and meet a marigold enthusiast

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison visit Lake Vyrnwy in Powys, exploring how industry and conservation work hand-in-hand to safeguard important habitats there. Ellie gets hands-on with woodland management in a bid to protect a rare day-flying moth, while Matt discovers the fascinating story of the Bank of the Black Ox. After a disappointing harvest, Adam Henson is hoping some new crops will make for an improved yield, while Charlotte Smith investigates concerns that the UK's environmental watchdogs aren't doing enough to protect wildlife and their habitats

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don harvests his pumpkins at Longmeadow, plants bulbs in a pot and has plenty of jobs for everyone to be getting on with over the coming weeks. Frances Tophill travels to Winkworth Arboretum in Surrey to celebrate the season and to explain the science behind the golden and red tones of autumn leaves. The team take inspiration from RHS Harlow Carr and take a look at their winter borders and the plants which will add interest and drama to gardens over the coming months

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond is in the Cairngorms hoping to see the annual red deer rut. Euan McIlwraith celebrates the colour red in the landscape and Arlene Stuart is picking acorns

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: Following gardeners in Orkney, Shetland and the Black Isle as they show how they have created stunning sites right on the very edges of Scotland in difficult conditions

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate British dairy products, preparing a classic cheese and onion turnover, a trinity cream and a number of traditional milk puddings

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Gregg and Chris are in Bromley with the Winbourne family, who desperately want to change their ways. Busy mum Clare and daughters Rachel and Sadie all rely on dad James to cook the family meals, but not everyone is satisfied with what's being rustled up. With the same meals on rotation and a lack of fruit and veg being consumed, the whole family are bored when it comes to mealtimes, and often resort to eating out

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A profile of actor Kenneth More, featuring archive interviews and clips capturing the milestones and highlights in his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Reach for the Sky (1956)
programme.desc: Biopic of Second World War hero Douglas Bader. A plane crash in 1931 cost the aspiring pilot both his legs, but after learning to walk on artificial limbs, he went on to become one of the RAF's most decorated heroes. However, more misfortune was in store when he was shot down over occupied France and taken prisoner. Starring Kenneth More, Muriel Pavlow and Alexander Knox

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Gavin & Stacey co-creator and star Ruth Jones traces her family tree, learning that her paternal grandfather was a leading figure in the Medical Aid Societies of South Wales - which provided a model for the National Health Service in 1948. On her maternal side, she discovers that her great-great-great-grandfather was a mariner who rose to the rank of captain in the domestic coastal trade

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Cherry Healey shed light on how some of Britain's biggest factories make the nation's favourite products, beginning with popular breakfast cereals. Gregg starts by receiving a boat-load of corn fresh from Argentina, and witnesses as it is cooked, milled and flavoured to become a top-selling cereal brand. Cherry learns about the immunity-boosting powers of vitamin D, which is added to many of the UK's most popular cereals, and historian Ruth Gordon samples a Victorian breakfast of lobster and pig's head, as part of a daily meal that included 4,500 calories

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Jenny Campbell and Tej Lalvani assess the profit-making potential of more business ideas, including a keen fisherman with a plan to market his own range of bait, a pasta home delivery service, and a brother and sister with a scheme to make money out of rubbish. Evan Davis presents

programme.title: A Life on Screen
programme.desc: The actor and writer looks back on his career in an in-depth interview recorded after he received a special award at the 2013 Baftas. The programme looks back at his varied television career, from his earliest collaborations with writing partner Terry Jones, his innovative breakthrough as one of the stars of Monty Python's Flying Circus, to his later dramatic roles and award-winning series of travelogues. Featuring contributions from those who have worked with Palin, including John Cleese, David Jason and Armando Iannucci

programme.title: Totally Under Control (2020)
programme.desc: An in-depth look at how the United States government handled the response to the Covid-19 outbreak during the early months of the pandemic. The film examines the decision-making that led the US to suffer the world's worst casualty rate during the crisis, with over 200,000 deaths. With contributions from public health officials

programme.title: Beatriz at Dinner (2017)
programme.desc: A holistic medicine practitioner visits a wealthy client at home, only to find herself stranded when her car breaks down. The client invites her to attend a dinner party she is throwing that night, where she is drawn into a battle of wills with her privileged fellow guests. Comedy drama, starring Salma Hayek and John Lithgow

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Monica develops new abilities with Micah's help, while Alejandro and his sister continue their trek to the US. Matt puts Molly's life in jeopardy when he asks her to locate the man in her dreams, and Claire becomes suspicious of her father. Drama, starring Dana Davis, Greg Grunberg and Adair Tishler

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Matt visits his father Maury and takes along Nathan as they search for answers regarding Molly's nightmare and the death threats received by Angela and Kaito. Meanwhile, a new hero appears and attempts to find Peter. Kristen Bell, Alan Blumenfeld and Adrian Pasdar star

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the audience. Presented by Fiona Bruce

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: Cultural review show offering a mixture of interviews, news features and performances from the world of the arts

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Host Sara Cox is joined by contributors Rebecca Front, Sophie Ward, Ade Adepitan and Bill Bailey to discuss this week's book group picks - Fifty-Fifty by Steve Cavanagh and Sophie Ward's Love and Other Thought Experiments

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: In Birmingham, the investigators crack down on the callous travel agent ripping off British holidaymakers, taking their money but never booking their flights. In light of the story of one limo driving a school party of 11-year-olds bursting into flames, there's also some spot-checking on luxury limos being driven in London

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore searches for tired and unwanted items at Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey she can transform from skip-bound to stylish. With three items saved, Sarah turns to upholsterer Sharon O'Connor to overhaul an old chair frame, while upcycler Jacqui Joseph attempts to transform a dated desk. Sarah's challenge is to update a set of four chairs. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Gregg and Chris are in Bromley with the Winbourne family, who desperately want to change their ways. Busy mum Clare and daughters Rachel and Sadie all rely on dad James to cook the family meals, but not everyone is satisfied with what's being rustled up. With the same meals on rotation and a lack of fruit and veg being consumed, the whole family are bored when it comes to mealtimes, and often resort to eating out

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: A team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len Goodman pays a visit to Scarborough-born actor Jimi Mistry, who recalls climbing into his family's Ford Capri as a child for a holiday to Castleton in the Peak District in 1979

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: The arrival of his brother Terry has caused a buzz all over Weld, but George is determined to warn everyone not to get too involved with him and his schemes

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the seven remaining contestants take part in a series of challenges as they return to the sewing room for reduce, reuse and recycle week. Joe Lycett kicks the group off with a pattern for a man's bomber jacket, which must be pieced together from four second-hand women's garments. In the transformation challenge, the sewers are tasked with changing laundry bags into a stylish and wearable garment, before finally being tasked to use old knitwear to make a made to measure jumper dress

programme.title: Britain and the Sea
programme.desc: Broadcaster David Dimbleby sails his yacht Rocket around the British coastline, exploring different aspects of the nation's maritime history, art and architecture along the way. He begins by sailing the south-west coast of Devon and Cornwall, where he tells the story of Sir Francis Drake and an array of adventurers, explorers, pirates and smugglers. He also welcomes record-breaking solo yachtswoman Ellen MacArthur on board and learns about one of the oldest maritime art forms of all - the tattoo, before agreeing to have one himself

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Huddersfield Town Hall, West Yorkshire, Nick Davies is drawn to a bobbin holder that reveals the town's textile heritage, while Christina Trevanion finds an incredible collection of glass scent bottles. Presenter, Paul Martin delves into the area's coal mining history

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Mark Billingham, Neil Delamere, AJ Odudu and Lucy Porter test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball is joined by the first couple to be eliminated from the competition after losing the dance-off, and there are interviews with the remaining pairs

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Blind adventurer Amar Latif travels along one of the region's most stunning river valleys, abseiling into a hidden gorge, canoeing across a reservoir, and meeting the local llamas while following a riverside trail along the Nidd

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair to answer questions on the specialist subjects of Vanessa Bell, Rachmaninov, Sherlock and David Bowie, before each is tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another first-round match as skiing enthusiasts the Apres Skiers take on co-worker the Colleagues. They must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Trevor, Simon, Schuster, and Garfunkel

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz begins, with two teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: The Children Act (2017)
programme.desc: As her marriage founders because of her dedication to her job, High Court judge Fiona Maye is given the case of a 17-year-old Jehovah's Witness who refuses on religious principle to undergo a potentially life-saving blood transfusion for leukaemia. Drama, starring Emma Thompson, Fionn Whitehead, Stanley Tucci and Jason Watkins

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: The Trump Show
programme.desc: The story of Donald Trump's fourth year in office, when the president, emboldened by his exoneration in the Russia investigation, invited the Taliban to Camp David and suggested buying Greenland from Denmark. It was also the year that Covid-19 spread around the world. In America, the virus killed more than 150,000 people by the middle of the year - yet Trump pushed back against government scientists. And when police officers killed George Floyd, the violent protests that followed forced Trump to an underground bunker. Now, as the election nears, the programme explores whether Trump has lost control of the narrative or whether his political-savvy fighting instincts will propel him to another victory

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison visit Lake Vyrnwy in Powys, exploring how industry and conservation work hand-in-hand to safeguard important habitats there. Ellie gets hands-on with woodland management in a bid to protect a rare day-flying moth, while Matt discovers the fascinating story of the Bank of the Black Ox. After a disappointing harvest, Adam Henson is hoping some new crops will make for an improved yield, while Charlotte Smith investigates concerns that the UK's environmental watchdogs aren't doing enough to protect wildlife and their habitats

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Crewmates Adam and Dan treat a patient who has fallen down a flight of stairs in a casino, while Rachel and Stuart respond to a call when a child falls from a bunk bed. As the shift continues, a distressing call from a patient threatening to jump into the river takes priority, and the call handler must try to keep the patient talking until help can arrive

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A special edition of the programme exploring what it takes to police Britain's roads, including footage of a Metropolitan Police operation to tackle moped-enabled crime

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roo Irvine and Danny Sebastian help two teams hunt for antiques at the West Midlands Showground in Shrewsbury, while Natasha Raskin Sharp tests the design knowledge of fairgoers

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades transforms a stained-glass door and window, Rachel Henderson takes an old fireplace, and Mark Haig attempts to inject new life into a collection of brass lighting

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don gives tips on how to look after wildlife in the months ahead, shows how to look after and protect tender garden plants and plans ahead for next year's harvest of fruit. Joe Swift finds out what it takes to look after a historic garden with a collection of heritage fruit trees in Hampshire, and Arit Anderson meets Fergus Garrett - head gardener at Great Dixter - to discover how the planting and garden management have increased biodiversity. Plus, the Staffordshire couple with a particular fondness for Japanese maples

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including St Helens v Wigan Warriors and Hull Kingston Rovers v Hull FC

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing's Len Goodman takes Baroness Tanni Grey-Thompson on a journey to Somerset to recreate a childhood holiday of 1980. The pair travel in a bright yellow Rover, just like her family car back in the day, and they visit the Cameley Lakes where the former Paralympian would have stayed. They also visit Longleat Safari park, go fishing, and visit the Wells Cathedral

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Weld receives another unexpected visitor, and George is convinced that Big Mac and Terry's new restaurant is changing the character of the town

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the six remaining contestants take part in a series of challenges as they return to the sewing room to revive the glamorous, and sometimes ridiculous style, of the 1980s. Patrick Grant and Esme Young challenge the sewers to get down to business with the trickiest pattern so far, a woman's power jacket, before they are tasked with turning high-vis workman clothes into a party outfit in the Transformation Challenge. Finally in the Made to Measure Challenge, the contestants create their own versions of a cocktail dress

programme.title: Britain and the Sea
programme.desc: David Dimbleby continues his voyage around Britain, sailing his yacht Rocket along coastal regions of the South East, the first point of attack for enemy forces and therefore essential to the nation's defence throughout history. Along the way, he takes a tour of HMS Victory, Nelson's flagship vessel from the Battle of Trafalgar, lovingly preserved in dry dock in Portsmouth. He also meets a carpenter who still makes wooden figureheads, discovers how a chamber pot acted as propaganda in the battle against Napoleon and reveals the way abstract art was used to deceive German submarine pilots

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Mark Billingham, Neil Delamere, AJ Odudu and Lucy Porter test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week, with a winner declared each Friday

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball chats to the lucky couple that survived the weekend's dance-off, and there is a closer look at the weekend's dances. Plus, more exclusive interviews and backstage gossip

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Radio 2's Janey Lee Grace and Tim Smith travel around the Cotswolds and the Malverns in search of antiques, assisted by experts James Braxton and David Harper. Janey's vintage Austin Healey takes her to uncover the story of cross-dressing music-hall superstar Vesta Tilley, while sports fan Tim - in a sporty MG - gets to grips with the art of Shin Kicking and the 'Cotswold Olympicks'

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham reports live on badger and fox families in the New Forest, while Michaela Strachan catches up with the latest from a seal breeding colony on the Isle of May. Iolo Williams goes in search of a wild parrot-like bird in a secret location in west Wales, and Gillian Burke discovers the surprising wildlife to be found amongst the chimney stacks and industrial ponds of South Yorkshire

programme.title: Aung San Suu Kyi: The Fall of an Icon
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the Myanmar leader's fall from grace. While under house arrest during the country's military dictatorship, she became a Nobel prize-winning champion of democracy, human rights and peaceful protest. However, since becoming the leader of her nation, she has been implicated in brutal atrocities against the Rohingya Muslims, and faced allegations of genocide at the International Court of Justice. Key figures in international politics discuss the reasons why the qualities that brought her such acclaim have been in marked contrast to the realities of her leadership

programme.title: Out of Her Mind
programme.desc: Sara organises a baby shower for Scoopy to prove she does not make everything about her, and is visited by a ghostly reminder of her own pregnancy

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: After advertising her home on Airbnb, Mandy takes in a guest from Russia - and they soon become more than friends. But can he be trusted? Comedy, written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan, with Tony Way (After Life)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Travelling Blind
programme.desc: Amar Latif is blind but that has never prevented him from travelling the world, having now visited more than a hundred countries and taken up skydiving, bungee jumping and skiing. But whenever Amar travels he relies on a sighted companion to guide him. In return he opens their eyes and their senses to a different way of travelling. In this one-off film, he is joined by comedian Sara Pascoe on a trip to Turkey, intending to go off the beaten track and explore the country's remote mountains and extraordinary mountains. However, it soon becomes clear they have very different ways of travelling and the journey becomes as much about their relationship as it is about the country

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: Laurence asks Kris to fight in a charity boxing match to raise cash for a homeless shelter. Can he make the leap from mild-mannered hotel manager to prizefighter? VIP guest Louise Brookes checks in, asking for the same room she stayed in 175 times while she attended the inquest into the Hillsborough disaster, and Ciara launches a new competition to promote weddings at the hotel

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: Harry Hill casts a comical eye over the history of police dramas, from the early days of Dixon of Dock Green to acclaimed modern shows like Line of Duty. He examines the familiar tropes that audiences know and love, as well as drawing attention to a few that have escaped audience's attention

programme.title: The Trump Show
programme.desc: Key players including Rudy Giuliani, Stormy Daniels, Steve Bannon and Ambassador John Bolton shed light on Trump's second year, as he faces challenges from his past

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Special episode looking at knife crime in the UK, including a dedicated police task force in Essex and a groundbreaking scheme in the West Midlands

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Mud, Sweat and Tractors: The Story of Agriculture
programme.desc: The documentary examining British food production during the 20th century looks at how the Hereford and Aberdeen Angus breeds of cattle have evolved over the years, since the nation earned the reputation of 'the Stockyard of the World' before the Second World War

programme.title: Climbing Blind
programme.desc: Documentary about Jesse Dufton and his attempt to take on the challenge of becoming the first blind person to lead a climb of the Old Man of Hoy in Orkney, Scotland. At four years of age, Jesse was diagnosed with retinitis pigmentosa, a rare genetic disease that breaks down the retina's cells. When he was 20, Jesse could no longer read. By the time he was 30, he could only detect light, with around a one to two per cent field of view. As a lifelong climber, what Jesse has achieved flies in the face of adversity, training for world cup events and leading traditional rock climbs with his sight guide and fiancee Molly. Now, his attempt on the Old Man of Hoy is testimony to his ambition to take on new and greater challenges, despite his devastating condition

programme.title: Final Ascent: The Legend of Hamish MacInnes
programme.desc: Documentary about the famous mountaineer, inventor of the all metal ice axe and author of the International Mountain Rescue Handbook. Suffering from delirium in his eighties, he lost his memories, but was able to recover them using pictures taken over the previous 60 years and copies of the books he had written

programme.title: Happy End (2017)
programme.desc: Following an industrial accident at one of their building sites, the Laurent family are joined by 12-year-old Eve as her mother lies close to death in hospital. Writer/director Michael Haneke's drama, starring Mathieu Kassovitz and Toby Jones

programme.title: Dracula by Northern Ballet
programme.desc: Bram Stoker's gothic romance is brought to life on stage in this ballet, performed by the dancers of Northern Ballet to music by Schnittke, Rachmaninov, Pärt and Daugherty. Choreographed by David Nixon OBE and recorded at Leeds Playhouse on Halloween 2019

programme.title: Mud, Sweat and Tractors: The Story of Agriculture
programme.desc: The documentary examining British food production during the 20th century looks at how the Hereford and Aberdeen Angus breeds of cattle have evolved over the years, since the nation earned the reputation of 'the Stockyard of the World' before the Second World War

programme.title: Climbing Blind
programme.desc: Documentary about Jesse Dufton and his attempt to take on the challenge of becoming the first blind person to lead a climb of the Old Man of Hoy in Orkney, Scotland. At four years of age, Jesse was diagnosed with retinitis pigmentosa, a rare genetic disease that breaks down the retina's cells. When he was 20, Jesse could no longer read. By the time he was 30, he could only detect light, with around a one to two per cent field of view. As a lifelong climber, what Jesse has achieved flies in the face of adversity, training for world cup events and leading traditional rock climbs with his sight guide and fiancee Molly. Now, his attempt on the Old Man of Hoy is testimony to his ambition to take on new and greater challenges, despite his devastating condition

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: The steeplejack tours unusual places of worship, beginning in Preston with one of Britain's tallest spires, and a Saxon church in Durham made from the remains of an abandoned Roman fort. He also visits St Paul's Cathedral and meets stonemasons involved in the restoration of York Minster

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross takes on one of his greatest challenges as he paints majestic mountains looming over a colourful seascape in only half an hour

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate a sketch of a dancer, which is believed to be the work of sculptor Auguste Rodin, and part of a series of drawings he made of a Cambodian dance troupe in 1906. The search for the truth takes them to Paris and Mexico and uncovers a forgery scandal that has rocked the French art establishment

programme.title: In Search of Dracula with Mark Gatiss
programme.desc: Mark Gatiss explores and celebrates the icon of popular culture. He begins at Orava Castle in Slovakia, as used in the classic vampire film Nosferatu, then heads to the London Library on the trail of Bram Stoker's newly discovered research literature. In Philadelphia, he studies Stoker's hand-written notes and examines the abandoned ideas, plotlines and characters that went on to become this world-famous story. Mark also meets with actors, experts and historians as he explores the Count's transition from page to screen - and even manages to reunite seven Hammer brides. Contributors include the latest Dracula, Danish actor Claes Bang

programme.title: CripTales
programme.desc: Fictional monologues based on factual research and the lived experiences of disabled people in Britain since 1970, beginning with Audition, written and performed by Mat Fraser. Mat sits in the waiting room before an audition, dreading how it will turn out

programme.title: CripTales
programme.desc: Liz Carr performs Tom Wentworth's monologue The Real Deal, in which a woman forges an unlikely friendship with a man she thinks is a benefits cheat

programme.title: Lucy Worsley's Fireworks for a Tudor Queen
programme.desc: The historian teams up with materials scientist Zoe Laughlin in an ambitious attempt to recreate one of the earliest and most spectacular firework displays in Britain. The extravagant festivities were originally laid on by Robert Dudley, a close friend of Queen Elizabeth I, as an attempt win the Virgin Queen's hand in marriage in 1575, and Lucy travels to Italy for clues on how to make the Tudor equivalent of a Catherine Wheel

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate a sketch of a dancer, which is believed to be the work of sculptor Auguste Rodin, and part of a series of drawings he made of a Cambodian dance troupe in 1906. The search for the truth takes them to Paris and Mexico and uncovers a forgery scandal that has rocked the French art establishment

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: Visits to historic places of work, including one of Britain's oldest dockyards in Chatham Kent, and Armley Mill in Leeds. At a 1,000-year-old tithe barn in Sussex, the steeplejack tries his hand at thatching, before getting a surprise on the top floor of the architecturally esteemed Lloyd's building in the City of London

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints a majestic snow-covered mountain, complete with rolling foothills, leafy trees, bushes and a serene lake

programme.title: Vikings
programme.desc: Neil Oliver tells the story of how the age of the Vikings came to an end, using archaeological evidence to explore the lives of the first Danish kings, the spread of Christianity in Scandinavia, and the voyages of discovery undertaken by Norse sailors in the Atlantic. He also profiles Canute, the former king of England, and reveals why the reputation he has accrued over the past millennium does a disservice to his diplomatic feats

programme.title: Blood of the Clans
programme.desc: When Bonnie Prince Charlie arrives in Scotland to regain the crown of his ancestors, the clans are called upon to rise in armed revolt for him. Many do, and they invade England, but others oppose him and join the British side to fight against the rebels. Some though play a cunning double game, pretending to be loyal to the British whilst secretly raising clansmen for the Prince. Presented by Neil Oliver

programme.title: Play for Today
programme.desc: Female workers at a textile factory clash with their own union. Drama by Colin Welland, starring Lynne Perrie and Elizabeth Spriggs. Originally broadcast in 1974

programme.title: DNA
programme.desc: The Minna case is reopened after Rolf and Neel find serious errors in the Danish DNA register, and Rolf and his former boss Astrid Oxlev assist the French police - with Claire Bobain (Charlotte Rampling) the lead investigator. In Danish, English, Polish and French

programme.title: DNA
programme.desc: With clues pointing to Poland, the team heads there to find a crucial witness in possession of a video, but Claire is concerned that Rolf seems more focused on his daughter's case than with the murder they are currently investigating. In Danish, English, Polish and French
