programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Blue Peter goes spooky for Halloween! Make your own bats and ghosts with wizarding author and Horrible Histories star Simon Farnaby and find out how Adam gets on joining the cast of Scream Street. Just how do those zombies, vampires and mummies really move?

programme.title: Out of This World
programme.desc: In this episode, Dr Maggie lifts the lid on the rovers, landers, orbiters and probes that have been sent to explore our universe and tells the incredible story of Rosetta, the spacecraft that tried to land on a comet. Dr Maggie’s star guest is Professor Andrew Coates, who talks about building a spacecraft to look for life on Mars. And Maggie answers viewers’ space questions and catalogues the top five longest-serving spacecraft.

programme.title: My Life

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: When the ball pit starts to overflow, the squad has to prevent headquarters from being destroyed

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: Two of Ms O's assistants accidentally find themselves on a mission

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: A special episode about the life of Mary, Queen of Scots, following her as she stumbles from young girl in Scotland to queen in France, back to queen in Scotland, and finally to Elizabeth I's arch enemy. Plus, the programme meets silver-nosed Danish scientist Tycho Brahe, and Mr H gives his unique take on the epic Battle of Lepanto in another edition of History's Craziest Fools

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve is on a mission to find the weirdest dinosaur ever

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and friends bounce around an underground trampoline park in Snowdonia

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Dr Chris puts Dr Xand inside a giant water balloon to show how viruses attack our bodies

programme.title: Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: Drama about a gifted schoolboy footballer, whose parents have recently split forcing him to move house and school. Louis Dunn stars

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: The brothers unveil their biggest and best enclosure yet, a huge new habitat for the Amur tiger sisters Aleena and Siberia. The boys test out the tigers new 80,000-gallon swimming pool and get hands-on as they prepare to move the world’s biggest cats. Meanwhile, the other animals at the park are treated to a bubble show, Tyler has to clean out the tapir toilet and the boys recall their travels to Kenya to see the world‘s last two remaining northern white rhinos.

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: It’s the zoo’s grand reopening and the boys are preparing to open the gates to the public for the first time. The night before, they were rudely awoken, and Moto the lion took the opportunity to investigate what the animals get up to during the dead of night. Cam draws the short straw and has to clean out the camel enclosure, while Billy the ocelot is given a pungent treat to play with and the boys recall the extraordinary story of how the zoo was started. Meanwhile, the brothers catch up on when they met with rangers on the front line training to intercept and arrest wildlife poachers.

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: Temperatures rise as the zoo is hit by a heatwave. Many of the animals are well adapted to the sun, but those from cooler climates need extra attention. The boys devise a series of ingenious tricks to keep each animal cool, including ice baths, mist showers and giant ice lollies. It’s fish flavour for the penguins, lettuce lollies for Marion the tortoise and a huge blood lolly for the big cats. Meanwhile, the camels get their yearly comb-down, Tyler has to muck out the stinky Penguin pool and the boys play with an orphaned elephant in Uganda.

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: The boys welcome some new arrivals to the zoo. Mimo the Pygmy Marmoset emerges from its nest box for the first time and two penguin chicks hatch. Meanwhile, it’s Tilly the red panda’s birthday, and to celebrate, the brothers decide to hold a cake-off. It’s a tradition at the zoo that when a new animal in born, all the animals get presents. Finally, we catch up with the boys in Rwanda as they meet wildlife heroes fighting to save the planet’s largest primates - the mountain gorilla.

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: It’s health check day and the boys give each of the animals a thorough MOT. Tamara the tapir gets a pedicure, Marion the tortoise gets a shell rub and the keepers discover a worrying lump on Luna the reindeer’s leg. It's Aaron’s turn to clean out the giant anaconda enclosure, and the boys venture to Uganda to ride with the country’s first animal ambulance.

programme.title: First Day

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po want to go outside, but it's raining

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Babies and their grown-ups play with plastic bottles, and enjoy songs and story time together

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The squirrels and Duggee go camping, but finding somewhere to pitch their tent proves more difficult that they expected

programme.title: School of Roars
programme.desc: There's monster trouble when the class forms a marching band. Meepa struggles to play the bogpipes and the other monsters squabble over who should be bandleader. Can Meepa master the puzzling pipes and restore harmony to the monster band?

programme.title: Let's Play
programme.desc: Sid steps through the magic curtain to become a dog walker and offers to take Mrs Mullet's canine Bert to the dog-grooming parlour - but things do not go according to plan

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: One day Pablo hears a strange sound and discovers his mother has some squeaky new shoes. He wants to know what they're trying to say. Are the shoes crying or laughing?

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Isla and Noah make paper pirate boats, complete with flags. However, they need some water to float them, so it's off to the park with Mum and Dad

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Tig goes on her first aeroplane trip, but her mother is worried

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: The Olobobs help Fozzybob find her way home with a special pair of glasses.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Youngster Larry invites his friends to his house for a meal of steamed tofu with egg and prawn, and vegetable-fried rice

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle has so much fun on a doggy playdate at Mr Willow's house that he decides to stay for a sleepover. However, the Brooklyn-Bells miss Waffle and cannot wait for his return. With the voice of Rufus Hound

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The lamb is paired with a sneezy Stripey for a boat-making project, and finds his partner's problem comes in handy for pushing their paper-hat creation to the finish line

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: The feisty seven-year-old and her classmates learn about the legend of the singing seals from Mr Mackie during a lesson on the beach. However, she cannot hear the creatures, and spends the next few days doing her upmost to get them to sing for her

programme.title: Melody
programme.desc: Melody listens to Zorba's Dance music while she is waiting for gingerbread men to cook

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee and Mo decide to have a camp out in the garden, but it seems there is always one more thing from his bedroom that Tee needs to help him fall asleep

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Q joins U to make some noise and create a vast array of new words

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘digger' and we go on a whirlwind word adventure

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: The animated adventures of friends who can always count on each other. Find out what happens when numbers come to life and how they really work and play with the Numberblocks

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny gets stuck in Padget's shop while playing a game of hide-and-seek with Flop

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Messy Monster cannot work out why his cart will not move, so he and Okido try to find out the answer

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friend paint a pot, and Artiste Tumble paints a portrait

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po drive to the Hidey Hup in the Tubby Car

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog and Pug enter the world of the theatre and help act out a shakespearean play.

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: With Ferne Corrigan and Rory Crawford

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Pre-school observational documentary following the lives of 8 young children and their families. Filmed at the contributors' eye level, this heart-warming series explores the diversity of everyday family life in the UK following the lives of 8 young children and their families

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Gem discovers Captain Sinker has claimed a new ship - one that actually floats - and has set off to plunder the high seas, leaving the Scarlet Squid to Captain Captain

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The squirrels set off for a jamboree to make some new friends, play games and have a picnic. They encounter another group of young animals with their very own version of Duggee, but everything they do is different - even Roly finds one of them too loud

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: The rabbit loses Cotton-tail's beloved squeaky toy, and must defeat his enemies to get it back

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: Andy heads to Africa to meet some meerkats and record their alarm call on his gizmo

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: A race across the desert slows to a crawl when Bitz and Purl over-engineer their vehicle

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: Grandma collects Tim from school and takes him for an eye test, where he is told he is short sighted. Tim chooses frames for his glasses with the help of Mummy and Topsy

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Animated fun for younger viewers featuring tales of the world's worst puppy who woofs and wags his way into the lives of an unassuming family

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: When JoJo hears the announcement of a new Captain Chloe film on the radio, she's disheartened to learn that it won't be available to watch for some time. Luckily, Gran Gran has a Gran Gran Plan - she and JoJo can make their own film to tide them over, and Jared (a keen filmmaker) can help. JoJo learns all about the camera, acting and editing, eventually producing an amazing film trailer for the gang to watch with popcorn.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Inspired by a trip to the art gallery, Sarah and Duck give Moon painting lessons

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: After discovering two paper crowns, Pepi Nana and Little Nana decide to be the Big Princess and the Little Princess. In Storyland the Little Princess goes to visit the Big Princess in her Big Castle, where she enjoys the biggest tea she has ever had

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: A space dustcart drops its load on the Clangers' planet, including something surprising.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Tombliboos' music makes everyone do things too fast. Igglepiggle and Upsy Daisy dance, while Makka Pakka washes stones - but when the music slows, so do they

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Game of Thrones and The Tudors actress Natalie Dormer tells a spooky tale about a little monster who learns being brave is better than being afraid

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Liverpool v West Ham United at Anfield, Burnley v Chelsea at Turf Moor and Sheffield United v Manchester City at Bramall Lane. The Reds were made to work hard for a 3-2 home win over the Hammers last season, while Chelsea and City will both have been hoping to emulate their away victories over their opponents in the previous campaign

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew Marr's guests include shadow justice secretary David Lammy MP, Jeremy Farrar, director of the Wellcome Trust and SAGE adviser, and Clément Beaune, French Europe minister. The newspapers are reviewed by Vicki Young, BBC deputy political editor, and Sarah Baxter, former deputy editor of The Sunday Times

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: All the latest political news from Wales and beyond, presented by James Williams

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow projects to make family homes out of a three-bed semi in Stoke-on-Trent, a split-level maisonette in Brixton, and a detached property in Surrey

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum visits Dundee, home of the Beano and the Dandy, to see how its comics tradition inspired a new version of the Bible. With music by Barbara Dickson and Cliff Richard

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Live Women's FA Cup Football
programme.desc: Everton v Manchester City (Kick-off 2.30pm). Gabby Logan presents all the action from the final at Wembley Stadium. Everton are looking to lift the trophy for the first time since the 2009/10 season and have impressed since the competition resumed with victories over Chelsea and Everton. The Citizens have overcome Leicester City and Arsenal since the restart and will now have high hopes of winning the trophy for the third successive season

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway goes on another walk around Wales in his efforts to get people to enjoy the great outdoors on their doorstep

programme.title: Coal House
programme.desc: The families preparations for the final concert are added to their already exhausting workload and consider their experiences so far. Nicola Reynolds narrates

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In the sixth annual event, the team heads out across the UK to lead rambles and meet inspirational youngsters who have been helped by the charity. Matt Baker is in the Peak District to take on challenge with 16-year-old Harvey, who was supported by bereavement counselling after his brother was killed, while Steve Brown is inspired to attempt scaling Wales' Pen y Fan mountain in his wheelchair after meeting 5-year-old Emerson, a sport-lover with a spinal-cord injury

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from the first two live weekends. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off before Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel-Horwood and Motsi Mabuse decide who will be the first pair to leave the contest. With music by Sam Smith

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness introduces Chris Harris and Freddie Flintoff to his latest creation - an all-electric, all-terrain ice-cream van for the 21st century. Elsewhere, the team tests the battery-powered Honda E, Vauxhall Corsa-e and Mini Electric with a race around a deserted Alton Towers

programme.title: Roadkill
programme.desc: Confronted about his affair, pressure mounts on the home front for Peter, while the party chairman asks him about his ambitions. Is he after the top job? Rochelle decides to pick up where Charmian left off

programme.title: Dark Land: Hunting the Killers
programme.desc: Documentary reinvestigating some of the country's most shocking unsolved murders, beginning with the case of 23-year-old Port Talbot woman Maureen Mulcahy, who was found strangled to death in February 1976. After a major breakthrough, the team discovers clues that suggest she may been the victim of a serial killer, and a new witness comes forward with new testimony

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: With double Olympic taekwondo champion Jade Jones, England rugby union winger Ugo Monye, gold medal-winning hockey star Sam Quek and former Manchester United defender Gary Pallister. Sue Barker asks the questions, with team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell

programme.title: Women's Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the final round of fixtures in the annual international rugby union tournament, featuring France v Ireland, Wales v Scotland and Italy v England

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Crewmates Adam and Dan treat a patient who has fallen down a flight of stairs in a casino, while Rachel and Stuart respond to a call when a child falls from a bunk bed. As the shift continues, a distressing call from a patient threatening to jump into the river takes priority, and the call handler must try to keep the patient talking until help can arrive

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the progress of a mid-terrace house in Chatham, Kent, a detached house in Bilston, West Midlands and a piece of land in Chesterton, Newcastle-under-Lyme

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A young man lies unconscious in his bedroom and it is up to an on-line friend in Texas to raise the alarm. Plus, a nine-year-old boy on holiday is stung by a giant jellyfish

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roo Irvine and Danny Sebastian help two teams hunt for antiques at the West Midlands Showground in Shrewsbury, while Natasha Raskin Sharp tests the design knowledge of fairgoers

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: The medical drama returns after a break due to the pandemic. The gang celebrates Jimmi's birthday, while Rob drives Karen and Jay up the wall, and Deborah encounters a stubborn mother-to-be

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin puts Tunbridge Wells and its property market to the test with his first- time buyers, who have £425,000 to invest in a starter home after saving hard for the deposit

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades transforms a stained-glass door and window, Rachel Henderson takes an old fireplace, and Mark Haig attempts to inject new life into a collection of brass lighting

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents a programme in which five dealers bid against one another to buy intriguing items brought in by members of the public. The opening edition features an unusual item of hairdressing memorabilia, something salvaged from a tip, a classic piece of industrial furniture and a beloved toy

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: Omar Hamdi exposes the potential risks of selling a car online when he investigates the case of a woman who claims she was duped out of her vehicle, while Steffan Powell discovers how agreeing to driving being monitored could lead to lower car insurance. Plus, the programme meets more Welsh holidaymakers whose flights were cancelled because of Covid-19 - can X-Ray chase down their refunds

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jack tells Denise that four of Ellie's properties have been raided by the police - but he is convinced Phil had something to do with it. Jay goes against Honey's wishes and tells Billy what happened, and when they discover that her attacker has been released on bail, Billy's emotions get the better of him. Rainie and Stuart are in dire need of a new member of staff. Luckily for them, Tiffany is in need of part-time work

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The Chinese video-sharing service was the social media sensation of lockdown, an app that provided a bit of fun for people stuck indoors and now has hundreds of millions of users. In this report, Tina Daheley investigates whether it is safe for the millions of young people who have signed up to use it

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Silent Witness star Liz Carr traces her family tree, learning about the life of her maternal grandfather, who died when her mother was just two. She also discovers that her paternal great-great-great-grandfather was involved in the attempted murder of a prominent landlord in Armagh in the 1850s

programme.title: Stand Up for Live Comedy
programme.desc: Jamali Maddix hosts the showcase for emerging comic talent from London, kicking things off with his experiences of 'original' Covid. He then introduces three guests, with Michael Odewale enlightening the audience about reverse revenge and black-on-black crime, Eleanor Tiernan admitting to overthinking everything and having a weird personality, and Helen Bauer rounds off the evening with tales of eating her own body weight in cheese

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: Peter Kay and Sian Gibson return for a one-off special episode of the comedy about the daily commute of supermarket employees John and Kayleigh, with the two stars taking the plunge and improvising the dialogue for the whole show

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Steph McGovern hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Miles Jupp and Sayeeda Warsi joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham is joined by Irish actor and singer Jessie Buckley, starring in Charlie Kaufman's Netflix drama I'm Thinking of Ending Things. Also appearing are comedian Bill Bailey - one of this year's Strictly contestants - plus fellow comic and children's author David Walliams. Singer Dermot Kennedy performs his single Giants, and the host offers a wry look at some trending news stories

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell explore a quirky cottage in Paddock Wood, Kent, a new build in Gnosall, Staffordshire, and a one-bedroom flat in Northampton

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: A fly-tipper who illegally dumped tonnes of waste in the grounds of an Oldham primary school for children with learning difficulties, leaving the school with a £22,000 bill

programme.desc: Omar Hamdi exposes the potential risks of selling a car online when he investigates the case of a woman who claims she was duped out of her vehicle, while Steffan Powell discovers how agreeing to driving being monitored could lead to lower car insurance. programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Gary Pe and Nick Hall help two teams search for items in the West Sussex town of Ardingly. Presenter Anita Manning also delves into restoration by learning how to gild a bauble

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Deborah is left out in the cold, and Sid may be the only one who can help her. Daniel, Zara and Harper make an uneasy threesome, and Bear tries to help a friend in need

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Presenter Steve Brown helps a bachelor find an ideal property in Guildford, Surrey, on a budget of £420,000

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and the team are at Wallyford Recycling Centre in East Lothian, where they transform a nest of tables, a tired trolley, and a telephone table

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against one another to buy intriguing items brought in by members of the public, including a selection of Hornby model railway equipment. Nigel Havers presents

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jay is amazed with Honey's strength after she talks to the police again, while Mick is unimpressed when Max offers Linda another shift at the restaurant. Kat comes up with a brainwave to sort out the Slaters' money woes - but Stacey and Kush are not best pleased with the idea. Tiffany is overwhelmed with the more morbid aspects of her new job at the funeral parlour but Rainie and Stuart do their best to make her feel welcome as only they can

programme.title: The Wedding Dress Shop
programme.desc: Samm helps a 60-something nurse who will retire and marry with just a few days in between. However, health difficulties may keep her from walking down the aisle unaided. Elsewhere, a younger bride's combination of big dreams and tight budget forces the team to get creative

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Porridge star Mark Bonnar, actress Sheila Hancock, actor Stephen Mangan and radio and TV presenter Anita Rani join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show in which participants attempt to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: Hannah's wedding day arrives, bringing everyone's stories together. David explores memories of his own marriage, Belle makes an unlikely alliance, Hannah confronts the question that has been plaguing her ever since Andy came back into her life, and Gail realises she may be the only one who can help

programme.title: Catch Her If You Can
programme.desc: Documentary following an attempt to expose a successful London businesswoman's double life as a con artist. A suspicious invoice tipped off one of her potential victims, and inspired her to track down other parties who had been fleeced by her in the past. Together, they came up with a plan to expose her misdeeds

programme.title: Rent Like a Boss
programme.desc: Big V and Tobias give their advice on outrageous rental issues, from dealing with nosy neighbours to landlords who do not respect their tenants' privacy. They also help a Manchester resident looking for a flatshare, who insists on bringing a caravan full of theatrical costumes with him to his new home

programme.title: US Election 2020
programme.desc: Katty Kay and Andrew Neil present rolling coverage of the US election result, as either Donald Trump or Joe Biden will be confirmed as president. Trump and vice president Mike Pence are hoping to hold onto the positions they have held for the Republican Party since the last election in 2016, while Barack Obama's former VP Biden and running mate Kamala Harris are looking to regain Democratic control of the White House

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: The McLeans' emotions are in turmoil as Caitlin's condition worsens, while Poppy is shocked when fake e-mails cause offence. Affairs of the heart prove problematic for Jac and Lou, and Angus urges Bob to fight for his marriage

programme.title: The Children's Hospital
programme.desc: Corran has a nervous wait to find out whether chemotherapy is working. Meanwhile, doctors fear Kloe may have fractured her skull after falling from a bunk bed, and the plastic surgeons help four-year-old Lois, who has suffered a dog bite, causing a deep cut to her nose

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: Hannah's wedding day arrives, bringing everyone's stories together. programme.title: US Election 2020

programme.title: US Election 2020
programme.desc: Katty Kay and Andrew Neil present rolling coverage of the US election result

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Liverpool v West Ham United at Anfield, Burnley v Chelsea at Turf Moor and Sheffield United v Manchester City at Bramall Lane. The Reds were made to work hard for a 3-2 home win over the Hammers last season, while Chelsea and City will both have been hoping to emulate their away victories over their opponents in the previous campaign

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew Marr's guests include shadow justice secretary David Lammy MP, Jeremy Farrar, director of the Wellcome Trust and SAGE adviser, and Clément Beaune, French Europe minister. The newspapers are reviewed by Vicki Young, BBC deputy political editor, and Sarah Baxter, former deputy editor of The Sunday Times

programme.title: Barra on the Bann
programme.desc: The final part of Barra Best's journey starts at the point where the Lower Bann begins at the northern side of Lough Neagh. He receives a rowing lesson from Coleraine's Olympic medal-winning brothers Peter and Richard Chambers, goes in search of the elusive otter, and reflects on the beauty, scale and surprise of Northern Ireland's longest river as his trip ends

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow projects to make family homes out of a three-bed semi in Stoke-on-Trent, a split-level maisonette in Brixton, and a detached property in Surrey

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum visits Dundee, home of the Beano and the Dandy, to see how its comics tradition inspired a new version of the Bible. With music by Barbara Dickson and Cliff Richard

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Live Women's FA Cup Football
programme.desc: Everton v Manchester City (Kick-off 2.30pm). Gabby Logan presents all the action from the final at Wembley Stadium. Everton are looking to lift the trophy for the first time since the 2009/10 season and have impressed since the competition resumed with victories over Chelsea and Everton. The Citizens have overcome Leicester City and Arsenal since the restart and will now have high hopes of winning the trophy for the third successive season

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers compete to design a garden for a family of seven in Stamford, Lincolnshire, who have just moved from rural Rutland and miss the countryside. With five noisy children, the parents want a peaceful garden that they can call their own, but the awkward plot gives the presenters a huge challenge, despite a budget of £4,500

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In the sixth annual event, the team heads out across the UK to lead rambles and meet inspirational youngsters who have been helped by the charity. Matt Baker is in the Peak District to take on challenge with 16-year-old Harvey, who was supported by bereavement counselling after his brother was killed, while Steve Brown is inspired to attempt scaling Wales' Pen y Fan mountain in his wheelchair after meeting 5-year-old Emerson, a sport-lover with a spinal-cord injury

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from the first two live weekends. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off before Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel-Horwood and Motsi Mabuse decide who will be the first pair to leave the contest. With music by Sam Smith

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness introduces Chris Harris and Freddie Flintoff to his latest creation - an all-electric, all-terrain ice-cream van for the 21st century. Elsewhere, the team tests the battery-powered Honda E, Vauxhall Corsa-e and Mini Electric with a race around a deserted Alton Towers

programme.title: Roadkill
programme.desc: Confronted about his affair, pressure mounts on the home front for Peter, while the party chairman asks him about his ambitions. Is he after the top job? Rochelle decides to pick up where Charmian left off

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, introducing highlights of Manchester United v Arsenal at Old Trafford and Tottenham Hotspur v Brighton & Hove Albion at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. Plus, action from Aston Villa v Southampton at Villa Park and Newcastle United v Everton at St James' Park

programme.title: Women's Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the final round of fixtures in the annual international rugby union tournament, featuring France v Ireland, Wales v Scotland and Italy v England

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Crewmates Adam and Dan treat a patient who has fallen down a flight of stairs in a casino, while Rachel and Stuart respond to a call when a child falls from a bunk bed. As the shift continues, a distressing call from a patient threatening to jump into the river takes priority, and the call handler must try to keep the patient talking until help can arrive

programme.title: Air an Smùid (Steaming)
programme.desc: A biting, cold winter delays exterior work on the puffer so Duncan keeps himself occupied by taking on the restoration of a vintage Ford truck. When work resumes on the VIC 27, the team sets to power-washing and sandblasting the hull. This removes decades worth of rust and scale - and leaves gaping great holes. The re-plating of the heavily corroded metal looks likely to be time consuming and costly

programme.title: Vets: Gach Creutair Beo
programme.desc: At Glasgow Vet School, Professor Donald Yool is in charge of a particularly risky operation on a Bichon Frise dog, to remove a tumour that is attached to her main blood vessel

programme.title: Luther 500: An t-Stri Spioradail (The Battle for Souls)
programme.desc: In a journey that takes him from Rome, Germany and Geneva to the Outer Hebrides and across the island of Ireland, Alex O'Henley uncovers why the Gaelic-speaking areas of Scotland became, to a large extent, Protestant while most of Ireland remained Catholic. He shows how Martin Luther's legacy still looms large over the Gaelic world

programme.title: ALT
programme.desc: Kim Carnie and Dol Eòin Mackinnon present footage from Idlewild's concert at Glasgow's QMU, plus sessions from Tamzene and Sacred Paws and an interview with James Grant

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo, thinking he's at the laundromat, wanders into a candy factory, where Fizz and Weasel are creating a bubblegum that makes Fizz-shaped bubbles.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Thinking he's hunting down a rat in his attic, Magoo finds himself on the theme park, devoted to... the glory of Fizz.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo comes across Fizz encased in ice and thinks he has found a prehistoric man. He thaws him out and acts like a caveman, so as not to distress him.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Global warming becomes a very real problem for a family trapped in a city that is being overtaken by rising ocean tides. With little time remaining, Gordon and Kayo race to the rescue. Adventure series, with the voices of David Menkin and Angel Coulby

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr. Bean is trying to impress some kids in the park with his football skills but winds up kicking their ball into a dilapidated, scary-looking house.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Su Pollard, Jordan Banjo, AJ Odudu and Jonathan Ross pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Martin and Roman Kemp team up to present the fun-packed show, featuring guests Bananarama, Katie Melua, Omid Djalili and Craig Revel Horwood

programme.title: Countrywise
programme.desc: Ben Fogle experiences the world beneath the Yorkshire Dales as he explores Britain's biggest underground cavern - Gaping Gill. Liz Bonnin visits Scotland's Forest Park of Galloway where she tracks one of our rarest mammals the pine martin. Plus, at home in the Cotswolds, actress Angela Griffin shares her love of local fresh produce with nine-year-old daughter Missey

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Joining Alan Titchmarsh to celebrate the great British countryside are Daniel O'Donnell and Clare Balding. Alan also meets a champion sheep herder and garden herbs expert. Carpenter Vikkie Lee is back with a new upcycling build and Alan tastes the best of British gin

programme.title: All Around Britain
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh and Alex Beresford go behind the scenes of The Warner Brothers Studio Tour - The Making of Harry Potter. Plus, a graffiti tour of Bristol, hometown of elusive street artist Banksy

programme.title: Love Your Garden Themed Specials
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh looks back on City Garden designs that he and the team have put into place, featuring a multi-purpose garden with a Jamaican feel, a contemporary family garden and a cottage garden in the heart of the city

programme.title: Nanny McPhee (2005)
programme.desc: A Victorian widower struggles to control his seven unruly children, who have already driven a series of childminders to distraction. But a strange-looking nanny with a few tricks up her sleeve soon gets the youngsters into line, leaving their weary father to concentrate on finding a new wife. Period fantasy adventure, starring Emma Thompson, Colin Firth and Kelly Macdonald

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. This time, a workman is crushed by a falling building, a woman loses her wig on a rollercoaster and a parachutist is knocked unconscious by the wing of a plane

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as another four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but anyone caught by the Chaser is out for good, leaving fewer players to team up and play in the final chase

programme.title: 5 Gold Rings
programme.desc: Simon Gregson (Corrie's Steve McDonald) and Matthew Wolfenden (Emmerdale's David Metcalfe) play to win more than £50,000 for their teammates in the game show hosted by Phillip Schofield. The soap stars answer visual questions by placing rings on an LED floor in five increasingly difficult stages

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which rugby legend Gareth Thomas, Good Morning Britain presenter Ranvir Singh and comedian London Hughes will need quick wits and nerves of steel to master the arcade-style machine

programme.title: Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo welcomes the Davies family from The Wirral, who take on the Russell family from Oxford. Both teams hope they have what it takes to win a £30,000 jackpot prize in the survey-based quiz

programme.title: The Pride of Britain Awards
programme.desc: Carol Vorderman hosts the ceremony celebrating the nation's unsung heroes who have selflessly done extraordinary things to help those in need this year. Joining them at the Royal Chelsea Hospital are celebrities and public figures, including Prince Charles, the Duke and Duchess of Cambridge, Captain Tom Moore, Ashley Banjo, David Beckham, Marcus Rashford, Ant and Dec, Stephen Fry and Olly Murs

programme.title: Exposure
programme.desc: Since 2015, anti-Muslim hate groups, conspiracy theories and hate crimes have all risen. Director Deeyah Khan meets the family of a Kansas farmer serving 30 years for an anti-Muslim bomb plot. She also films with a right wing, armed militia who believe in conspiracy theories that Muslims are trying to take over America

programme.title: Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Jill Douglas is joined by Ugo Monye and Gareth Thomas to present action from Italy v England, France v Ireland and Wales v Scotland in the final round of fixtures

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents action from Oulton Park, featuring from round eight of the Ginetta GT4 Championship and round nine of the British F4 Championship

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinser as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Motley Crue drummer Tommy Lee discusses his lockdown TikTok fame and plea for Trump not to ban the Chinese-owned app, new face tattoos and album Andro

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Judge Rinder's Crime Stories
programme.desc: Robert Rinder examines the murders of police officers Nicola Hughes and Fiona Bone in 2012, hearing about their fathers' efforts to fundraise in their memories

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five members of a family from Lancashire answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Nikki, Jeraint, Sasha and Alan pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Charity is in a pickle, Moira faces a dark chapter from her past, and Cain squares up in the village

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emma confesses to Alina that she accepted a share of the bistro robbery money and gave it to Steve to help Oliver. Abi is suspicious when Debbie makes out she is a high-flyer and is due in town for a work meeting. Michael is shocked to see Grace at the hospital, flanked by two prison officers

programme.title: Coronation Street: Soapy Slip Ups
programme.desc: A look at out-takes from the programme's archives, featuring misbehaving props, wardrobe malfunctions, corpsing cast members and missed lines, with stars Simon Gregson, Jimmi Harkishin, Ben Price, Jane Danson, Alex Mardell, Jack P Shepherd and Jenny McAlpine talking about their biggest bloopers

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: When Abi mentions what a nasty piece of work Ray Crosby turned out to be, Debbie's intrigued. Michael confides in James that Grace is pregnant, and the police question Emma about the cash she received from Scott

programme.title: Don't Rock the Boat
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and AJ Odudu present as 12 celebrities set out to row the entire length of Britain, with the two teams of six battling it out over more than 500 miles. In the first instalment, Tom Watson, Denise Lewis, Jodie Kidd, Craig Charles, Fleur East, Joe Weller, Jack Fincham, Lucy Fallon, Victoria Pendleton, Shaun Wallace, Adam Thomas and Kimberly Wyatt are split into two crews before facing their first epic leg at sea by rowing 90 nautical miles from St Ives to Pembroke, battling sea sickness and exhaustion on this 30-hour journey

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo, thinking he's at the laundromat, wanders into a candy factory, where Fizz and Weasel are creating a bubblegum that makes Fizz-shaped bubbles.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Thinking he's hunting down a rat in his attic, Magoo finds himself on the theme park, devoted to... the glory of Fizz.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo comes across Fizz encased in ice and thinks he has found a prehistoric man. He thaws him out and acts like a caveman, so as not to distress him.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Global warming becomes a very real problem for a family trapped in a city that is being overtaken by rising ocean tides. With little time remaining, Gordon and Kayo race to the rescue. Adventure series, with the voices of David Menkin and Angel Coulby

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr. Bean is trying to impress some kids in the park with his football skills but winds up kicking their ball into a dilapidated, scary-looking house.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Su Pollard, Jordan Banjo, AJ Odudu and Jonathan Ross pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Martin and Roman Kemp team up to present the fun-packed show, featuring guests Bananarama, Katie Melua, Omid Djalili and Craig Revel Horwood

programme.title: Countrywise
programme.desc: Ben Fogle experiences the world beneath the Yorkshire Dales as he explores Britain's biggest underground cavern - Gaping Gill. Liz Bonnin visits Scotland's Forest Park of Galloway where she tracks one of our rarest mammals the pine martin. Plus, at home in the Cotswolds, actress Angela Griffin shares her love of local fresh produce with nine-year-old daughter Missey

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Joining Alan Titchmarsh to celebrate the great British countryside are Daniel O'Donnell and Clare Balding. Alan also meets a champion sheep herder and garden herbs expert. Carpenter Vikkie Lee is back with a new upcycling build and Alan tastes the best of British gin

programme.title: All Around Britain
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh and Alex Beresford go behind the scenes of The Warner Brothers Studio Tour - The Making of Harry Potter. Plus, a graffiti tour of Bristol, hometown of elusive street artist Banksy

programme.title: Love Your Garden Themed Specials
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh looks back on City Garden designs that he and the team have put into place, featuring a multi-purpose garden with a Jamaican feel, a contemporary family garden and a cottage garden in the heart of the city

programme.title: Nanny McPhee (2005)
programme.desc: A Victorian widower struggles to control his seven unruly children, who have already driven a series of childminders to distraction. But a strange-looking nanny with a few tricks up her sleeve soon gets the youngsters into line, leaving their weary father to concentrate on finding a new wife. Period fantasy adventure, starring Emma Thompson, Colin Firth and Kelly Macdonald

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. This time, a workman is crushed by a falling building, a woman loses her wig on a rollercoaster and a parachutist is knocked unconscious by the wing of a plane

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as another four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but anyone caught by the Chaser is out for good, leaving fewer players to team up and play in the final chase

programme.title: 5 Gold Rings
programme.desc: Simon Gregson (Corrie's Steve McDonald) and Matthew Wolfenden (Emmerdale's David Metcalfe) play to win more than £50,000 for their teammates in the game show hosted by Phillip Schofield. The soap stars answer visual questions by placing rings on an LED floor in five increasingly difficult stages

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars

programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo welcomes the Davies family from The Wirral, who take on the Russell family from Oxford. Both teams hope they have what it takes to win a £30,000 jackpot prize in the survey-based quiz

programme.title: Tomb Raider (2018)
programme.desc: After her father's disappearance, kickboxing heiress Lara Croft makes money as a bike courier and refuses to claim her inheritance, convinced her dad is still alive. But when she learns the country estate could be sold off unless she claims it, she reluctantly agrees - and finds a pre-recorded message from her father giving clues to his possible whereabouts. Determined to find him, she sets out across the sea to Hong Kong - but soon crosses paths with a small army of mercenaries determined to break into a supposedly haunted tomb. Action adventure based on the video game, starring Alicia Vikander, Dominic West, Walton Goggins and Kristin Scott Thomas

programme.title: Tomb Raider (2018)
programme.desc: After her father's disappearance, kickboxing heiress Lara Croft makes money as a bike courier and refuses to claim her inheritance, convinced her dad is still alive. But when she learns the country estate could be sold off unless she claims it, she reluctantly agrees - and finds a pre-recorded message from her father giving clues to his possible whereabouts. Determined to find him, she sets out across the sea to Hong Kong - but soon crosses paths with a small army of mercenaries determined to break into a supposedly haunted tomb. Action adventure based on the video game, starring Alicia Vikander, Dominic West, Walton Goggins and Kristin Scott Thomas

programme.desc: Jonah and Amy travel to her childhood home to help her parents move out. Glenn and Garrett vie for the Cloud 9 Integrity Award, while Dina uses Mateo's relationship with Jeff to fix problems within the store. America Ferrera stars

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Mateo's undocumented status causes trouble once again, while Glenn and Amy do battle with an online troll. Jonah and Cheyenne provide legal counsel for Mateo - though it could prove unhelpful. Comedy, starring America Ferrera

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons try to impress professional footballer Osebi in Manchester, who then goes on a date with the person whose taste in clothes he liked best

programme.title: Dinner Date
programme.desc: Culinary-themed dating series. This time, Kirby from London chooses three men to date based on the menus they have put together. After the dates are done and dusted, she can only choose to see one of them again, but who will it be?

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep

programme.title: Dinner Date

programme.title: Dress to Impress

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness offers one last group of men the chance to enter the 'love lift' and win the hearts of his 30 single ladies. Tonight's would-be Romeos are Jono, a musical theatre performer from Mansfield, City trader Andrew from London, Blackpool car salesman Ian, and Jon, an account executive from Milton Keynes. Plus, Paddy catches up with last week's couples on the Isle of Fernandos

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring everything from giddy grandmas, cute kids and crazy animals to below-the-belt mishaps, practical jokers and painful-looking accidents

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Jake returns from the base with his girlfriend Tammy, and asks Walden for money so he can propose to her. However, the teenager has misunderstood his relationship with the tattoo shop owner, who has several children and just wants a bit of fun with no strings attached. programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan tries to make Valentine's Day special for Lindsay, but his plans go awry at the last minute. Meanwhile, with Kate back in town, Walden tries to persuade her to take him back. Comedy, starring Ashton Kutcher, Jon Cryer and Angus T Jones

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Taylor Swift agrees to go to a homecoming dance with Chris after he asks her via online video. However, when she starts to fall for him, her music takes a hit. Meanwhile, Peter decides to try a new profession and sees firsthand what it's like to be an Uber driver

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: In order to 'save' Stewie from autism, Peter and Lois decide not to vaccinate him and then proceed to convince the rest of Quahog to follow suit. Stewie runs away to save himself from diseases until Sean Penn swoops in to save the day and brings vaccines to the whole town

programme.title: Don't Hate the Playaz
programme.desc: Maya Jama, Big Zuu and Dane Baptiste go up against Lady Leshurr, Raye Darren Harriott in rap battles, dance-offs and freestyling challenges. Jordan Stephens hosts, with help from DJ Shortee Blitz on the decks. Jordan Stephens hosts

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois and her friends take a shine to hunky reporter Dallas, who looks set to replace Tom Tucker as news anchor, Brian pretends to be a millionaire to impress a new love interest

programme.title: Family Guy

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan is excited when he is put in charge of the CIA's number one fundraiser - the annual calendar. Meanwhile, Roger decides to go to university, but his plans get derailed when he has to fake his death to avoid paying a huge balance on a credit card

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Dan unwittingly puts Amelia in danger, Meena receives a surprise gift, and Nate is hopeful

programme.title: Carry On Dick (1974)
programme.desc: Two officers of the law dedicated to catching a cheeky highwayman enlist the help of a vicar, unaware the pious pastor and his buxom housemaid are not as innocent as they seem to be. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Jack Douglas, Barbara Windsor, Bernard Bresslaw, Joan Sims and Hattie Jacques

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate when a man collapses and dies outside a pub after a fight with his brother-in-law. The discovery of suspicious burn marks on the victim's forehead reveals sinister secrets and when a second body is found, the detectives are drawn into a world of superstition and family tensions. Murder mystery with John Nettles, John Hopkins and guest starring Geoffrey Whitehead

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the apparent suicide of a solicitor rumoured to have been stealing money from clients. When rival lawyer Otto Benham is later found murdered in a bizarre manner, the detectives begin to suspect the two deaths may be related. Mystery, starring John Nettles, John Hopkins, Barry Jackson, James Weber Brown and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Schoolteacher Connie Bishop keeps waking in the night, convinced she has an intruder, but most of the villagers in Badger's Drift dismiss her fears as an overactive imagination. Days later, a local councillor thought to have been obsessed with her is found murdered in her garden, and the ensuing investigation by Barnaby and Jones uncovers jealous rivals, ruthless social climbers and a cottage with a few secrets. Guest starring Paul Kaye, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Belgravia
programme.desc: Anne and James are at loggerheads following the revelations at the Brockenhursts' party. With Oliver fixated on discovering the origins of his father's new protege, Susan uses the opportunity to plan a covert trip to meet with a new friend, and enlists her maid Speer to keep the true nature of the rendezvous under wraps. Meanwhile, Lady Templemore begins work on her lavish plans for her daughter's wedding, but much to her disappointment, Lady Maria seems fixated on someone other than her fiance

programme.title: Belgravia
programme.desc: John appeals to members of the Trenchards' staff in his bid to uncover the truth about Charles, while Turton and Ellis are quick to betray their employers and prove surprisingly adept at deceit and spying. Susan makes a seemingly miraculous discovery, and begrudgingly agrees to accompany Anne and James to their country estate in the hope of extracting further information about Oliver. Stephen makes a last-ditch attempt to secure the funds he needs to pay off his debt - a deal that puts his life in danger

programme.title: The Loch
programme.desc: The search for a serial killer in the area around Loch Ness becomes a matter of life and death for local detective Annie Redford, who is trying to cope with her first murder case. When the body of a local man is found at the foot of Carn Mohr Mountain and an isolated human heart on the loch shore, the town's normality is shattered and the nightmare begins. Thriller, starring Laura Fraser and Siobhan Finneran

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The last run of the detective drama ended with Lewis (Kevin Whately) starting a new life away from the force, but it seems that peace and quiet doesn't suit him - which is just as well, as newly promoted DI Hathaway (Laurence Fox) is struggling to find a sidekick, and within just four weeks is already on to his second sergeant, DS Lizzie Maddox (Angela Griffin). So, Chief Superintendent Innocent (Rebecca Front) decides to reunite the former partners to look into the murder of a neurosurgeon, a case with potential links to the worlds of animal rights and blood sports. In the first of this two-part story, suspicion falls on glamorous widow Erica (Kara Tointon), but it turns out there are plenty of other people in the victim's inner circle who were driven by fear and loathing

programme.title: The Royal Wives of Windsor
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ahead of the wedding of Prince Harry and Meghan Markle, this documentary explores how the women who have married into the house of Windsor over the last century have transformed one of the most famous dynasties in the world. Historians including David Starkey, Kate Williams and Piers Brendon, broadcasters Jeremy Paxman and Gyles Brandreth, biographers Andrew Morton, Penny Junor and Anne Sebba, royal insiders and journalists provide a review of British social history as reflected through the lives of the royal wives of Windsor

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The family return from holiday, and it seems as if they never went away. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.desc: Maureen knows Bill is to be evicted and wonders why he has not mentioned it. Roy surprises Gail and Martin by agreeing to buy the cafe. Judy discovers she is not pregnant

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Judy tells Gary she is not pregnant and he wonders if he is sterile. Deirdre feels Ken is taking it for granted that she will move in with him

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned is angry with Jan for forcing Jack to leave. Kim invites Sophie to come with her to New Zealand. Zoe tells Sophie that if she leaves she need not come back

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sophie tells Zoe she has changed her mind about not wanting a baby with her. Sam decides to give himself up to the police. Viv flirts with Paddy when they get drunk together

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police are called in to investigate when a local judge is the target of a series of mysterious attacks, but the case takes a sinister turn when the prime suspect is found dead on his own doorstep. Peggy is delighted when David's new radio starts picking up police broadcasts, but a news reporter doesn't share her enthusiasm. Rural drama, with Gwen Taylor and David Lonsdale

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid arranges a fishing weekend, blissfully unaware Jean's father is coming to stay. Classic family comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The rural drama returns after a winter break. A woman receives a shock when a man claiming to be her dead husband surfaces in the village and attempts to pick up the threads of his former life - though the locals aren't convinced of his identity. Lord Ashfordly's behaviour takes a strange turn after he eats some of Peggy's peculiar batch of mushrooms. Gwen Taylor and Rupert Vansittart star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak, Mandy and Butch return from Australia, the Dingles descend on the court just in time for Sam's hearing, and Jack moves into Annie's cottage

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lisa tries to motivate the Dingles into getting jobs. Robert refuses to spend time with his dad. Sarah finds out that Kathy and Jan both knew about Jack's affair before she did

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken and Sue are embarrassed when Deirdre finds them in bed together. Gail is annoyed that the Baldwins have not taken Roy's offer seriously

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike cannot believe Alma has turned down Roy's offer, and Sue gives Ken an ultimatum. Sally is not happy about working with counterfeit goods and confides in Kevin

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The naked body of a business executive is found dumped in a rival factory's steriliser machine, leaving Barnaby and Scott with a complicated mystery to unravel. Their investigation begins with factory owners the Plummers, who seem to be hiding more than their share of secrets. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, John Hopkins, Annette Crosbie and Jasper Britton

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An aristocrat's wealthy, overbearing wife finances a North African archaeological dig to find the skull of John the Baptist. Poirot is in attendance, but when the woman is found stabbed to death, and only her husband, the exuberant Lord Boynton, appears distraught, the detective struggles to identify the killer among the wealth of suspects. Guest starring Tim Curry, Cheryl Campbell, John Hannah, Emma Cunniffe, Mark Gatiss, Angela Pleasence and Beth Goddard, with David Suchet in the title role

programme.title: White House Farm
programme.desc: Six-part drama based on the true story of the mass murder of a family in an Essex farmhouse in August 1985, providing a fresh insight into these tragic events. In the early hours of August 7th 1985, Nevill and June Bamber were shot and killed inside their farmhouse, along with their adoptive daughter, Sheila Caffell, and Sheila's six-year-old twin sons, Daniel and Nicholas Caffell. Police receive a call from the only surviving member of June and Nevill's immediate family, their adoptive son Jeremy. At first, it appears to be an open-and-shut case to investigating officer DCI Thomas 'Taff' Jones, but for DS Stan Jones, something does not feel right

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An aristocrat goes missing, along with his collection of explicit erotic paintings - and Morse's investigation provides him with a close-up view of the upper crust's dark side. What is more, there is a further culture shock lying in wait - the prospect of working with a new female pathologist. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Amanda Hillwood, Patricia Hodge and Clifford Rose

programme.desc: The age gap between Sid and his children becomes apparent when he returns home early from work. Comedy, starring Sid James

Rural drama, starring Vanessa Hehir and Lauren Drummond

programme.desc: Butch is nervous on his first day of work at Home Farm. Lisa riles Zak when she insists he gets a job. Tony and Becky are stunned when Emma reveals the identity of Geri's father

programme.desc: Jack's absence is causing problems on the farm. Tony asks Emma whether Greg forced her into having sex. Sophie has a difficult conversation with Zoe

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alec looks forward to taking Rita to lunch. Sean is furious with Des for double-crossing him. Liz is stunned when Fraser turns up at The Hourglass

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The discovery of a priceless music score leads to startling revelations about its composer's life. programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby becomes an unwelcome guest at baron Ned Fitzroy's wedding to Beth Porteous when the maid of honour is found pinned to the wall with a dagger. Meanwhile, Cully gets cold feet about tying the knot with Simon and is disappointed to learn the honeymoon to New Zealand will have to be cut short. With his own daughter's nuptials to attend, Barnaby races to unearth the family secrets he is sure lie behind the Fitzroy tragedy. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Laura Howard

programme.title: White House Farm
programme.desc: Stan and his number two Mick Clark speak to Jeremy and relatives of the murdered family, but their picture of what really happened at White House Farm remains inconclusive. The twins' father Colin Caffell is devastated by his loss, and is initially comforted by Jeremy, while post-mortem examinations reveal nothing to contradict the murder-suicide theory. Meanwhile, relative Ann Eaton cannot believe Sheila was responsible, and discusses her growing suspicions with her brother David Boutflour

programme.title: Inspector Morse

programme.title: The Royal Wives of Windsor
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ahead of the wedding of Prince Harry and Meghan Markle, this documentary explores how the women who have married into the house of Windsor over the last century have transformed one of the most famous dynasties in the world. Historians including David Starkey, Kate Williams and Piers Brendon, broadcasters Jeremy Paxman and Gyles Brandreth, biographers Andrew Morton, Penny Junor and Anne Sebba, royal insiders and journalists provide a review of British social history as reflected through the lives of the royal wives of Windsor

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The family return from holiday, and it seems as if they never went away. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Dan unwittingly puts Amelia in danger, Meena receives a surprise gift, and Nate is hopeful

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maureen knows Bill is to be evicted and wonders why he has not mentioned it. Roy surprises Gail and Martin by agreeing to buy the cafe. Judy discovers she is not pregnant

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Judy tells Gary she is not pregnant and he wonders if he is sterile. Deirdre feels Ken is taking it for granted that she will move in with him

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned is angry with Jan for forcing Jack to leave. Kim invites Sophie to come with her to New Zealand. Zoe tells Sophie that if she leaves she need not come back

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sophie tells Zoe she has changed her mind about not wanting a baby with her. Sam decides to give himself up to the police. Viv flirts with Paddy when they get drunk together

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police are called in to investigate when a local judge is the target of a series of mysterious attacks, but the case takes a sinister turn when the prime suspect is found dead on his own doorstep. Peggy is delighted when David's new radio starts picking up police broadcasts, but a news reporter doesn't share her enthusiasm. Rural drama, with Gwen Taylor and David Lonsdale

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid arranges a fishing weekend, blissfully unaware Jean's father is coming to stay. Classic family comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The rural drama returns after a winter break. A woman receives a shock when a man claiming to be her dead husband surfaces in the village and attempts to pick up the threads of his former life - though the locals aren't convinced of his identity. Lord Ashfordly's behaviour takes a strange turn after he eats some of Peggy's peculiar batch of mushrooms. Gwen Taylor and Rupert Vansittart star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak, Mandy and Butch return from Australia, the Dingles descend on the court just in time for Sam's hearing, and Jack moves into Annie's cottage

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lisa tries to motivate the Dingles into getting jobs. Robert refuses to spend time with his dad. Sarah finds out that Kathy and Jan both knew about Jack's affair before she did

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken and Sue are embarrassed when Deirdre finds them in bed together. Gail is annoyed that the Baldwins have not taken Roy's offer seriously

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike cannot believe Alma has turned down Roy's offer, and Sue gives Ken an ultimatum. Sally is not happy about working with counterfeit goods and confides in Kevin

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The naked body of a business executive is found dumped in a rival factory's steriliser machine, leaving Barnaby and Scott with a complicated mystery to unravel. Their investigation begins with factory owners the Plummers, who seem to be hiding more than their share of secrets. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, John Hopkins, Annette Crosbie and Jasper Britton

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An aristocrat's wealthy, overbearing wife finances a North African archaeological dig to find the skull of John the Baptist. Poirot is in attendance, but when the woman is found stabbed to death, and only her husband, the exuberant Lord Boynton, appears distraught, the detective struggles to identify the killer among the wealth of suspects. Guest starring Tim Curry, Cheryl Campbell, John Hannah, Emma Cunniffe, Mark Gatiss, Angela Pleasence and Beth Goddard, with David Suchet in the title role

programme.title: White House Farm
programme.desc: Six-part drama based on the true story of the mass murder of a family in an Essex farmhouse in August 1985, providing a fresh insight into these tragic events. In the early hours of August 7th 1985, Nevill and June Bamber were shot and killed inside their farmhouse, along with their adoptive daughter, Sheila Caffell, and Sheila's six-year-old twin sons, Daniel and Nicholas Caffell. Police receive a call from the only surviving member of June and Nevill's immediate family, their adoptive son Jeremy. At first, it appears to be an open-and-shut case to investigating officer DCI Thomas 'Taff' Jones, but for DS Stan Jones, something does not feel right

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An aristocrat goes missing, along with his collection of explicit erotic paintings - and Morse's investigation provides him with a close-up view of the upper crust's dark side. What is more, there is a further culture shock lying in wait - the prospect of working with a new female pathologist. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Amanda Hillwood, Patricia Hodge and Clifford Rose

Rural drama, starring Vanessa Hehir and Lauren Drummond

programme.desc: Jack's absence is causing problems on the farm. Tony asks Emma whether Greg forced her into having sex. Sophie has a difficult conversation with Zoe

programme.desc: Alec looks forward to taking Rita to lunch. Sean is furious with Des for double-crossing him. Liz is stunned when Fraser turns up at The Hourglass

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The discovery of a priceless music score leads to startling revelations about its composer's life. As Barnaby and Scott investigate, they discover the manuscript may also provide them with the clue they need to solve the case of a local man found battered to death in his cottage. Guest starring June Whitfield and Trevor Peacock

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby becomes an unwelcome guest at baron Ned Fitzroy's wedding to Beth Porteous when the maid of honour is found pinned to the wall with a dagger. Meanwhile, Cully gets cold feet about tying the knot with Simon and is disappointed to learn the honeymoon to New Zealand will have to be cut short. With his own daughter's nuptials to attend, Barnaby races to unearth the family secrets he is sure lie behind the Fitzroy tragedy. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Laura Howard

programme.title: White House Farm
programme.desc: Stan and his number two Mick Clark speak to Jeremy and relatives of the murdered family, but their picture of what really happened at White House Farm remains inconclusive. The twins' father Colin Caffell is devastated by his loss, and is initially comforted by Jeremy, while post-mortem examinations reveal nothing to contradict the murder-suicide theory. Meanwhile, relative Ann Eaton cannot believe Sheila was responsible, and discusses her growing suspicions with her brother David Boutflour

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The master of a prestigious college asks Morse to investigate the disappearance of one of his colleagues. The subsequent discovery of a dismembered torso soon has everyone fearing the worst - until the missing man turns up alive and well. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, James Grout, Beatie Edney, Sian Thomas and Barry Foster

programme.title: Hornblower
programme.desc: First episode of the drama based on CS Forester's tales of an 18th-century seafaring adventurer.
